prompts.text: Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST webhook at /contacts, validates the email field is present, and returns a success response with the contact ID.
Create a workflow triggered by a cron schedule every day at 9am that fetches a list of users from an API endpoint and logs the count.
Create a workflow with an HTTP GET endpoint at /health that checks a database connection status and returns either "healthy" or "unhealthy" with the current timestamp.

Create a workflow that receives a POST webhook at /transform with a JSON body containing a "text" field, converts it to uppercase, counts the words, and returns both the transformed text and word count.

Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST at /orders with order data. Route the order based on its type: "subscription" orders go through a recurring billing setup, "one-time" orders go through direct payment processing, and "trial" orders skip payment and create a trial account. Each path returns a different confirmation message.

Create a workflow triggered by a POST webhook at /support-tickets. Classify the ticket priority as "critical", "high", "medium", or "low" based on the severity field. Critical tickets send an immediate alert and escalate to a manager. High tickets assign to senior support. Medium and low tickets go to the general queue. All tickets get a confirmation email sent.

Create a workflow with an HTTP POST endpoint at /leads that receives lead data. First validate the required fields (name, email, company). If validation fails, return an error. If valid, check if the lead already exists by email. Existing leads get updated, new leads get created. Both paths then trigger a notification to the sales team with the lead details.

Create a workflow with a cron trigger every hour that fetches pending invoices. For each invoice, check if it is overdue. Overdue invoices get a reminder email sent and their status updated to "reminded". Non-overdue invoices within 3 days of due date get a courtesy reminder. All others are skipped. Return a summary of actions taken.

Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST at /onboarding with new employee data. First, create accounts in parallel: email account, Slack workspace, and project management tool. After all three accounts are created, send a welcome email with all the account details. Then set a 7-day timer wait. After the wait, send a follow-up survey to check how onboarding went.

Create a workflow triggered by POST at /approval-request that handles a multi-stage approval process. First, validate the request data. Then send an approval request to the first approver and wait for their webhook response. If approved, send to a second approver and wait again. If the second approver also approves, execute the approved action and notify the requester. If either approver rejects, notify the requester of the rejection with the reason. Track the full approval chain.

Create a workflow with a POST endpoint at /batch-import that receives an array of product items. Loop through each item and for each one: validate the data format, check for duplicates, transform prices to the correct currency, and categorize the product. Items that fail validation go to an error collection. Valid items are inserted into the database. After all items are processed, generate a summary report with counts of successful imports, duplicates found, and validation errors.

Create a workflow triggered by a cron every Monday at 8am and also by an HTTP POST at /reports/generate. The workflow gathers data from three sources in parallel: sales metrics, customer feedback scores, and support ticket statistics. After all data is collected, merge the results and generate a formatted report. Then branch based on a threshold: if the overall score is above 80, send a congratulations message to the team channel. If between 60-80, send a regular update. If below 60, escalate to management with action items. Finally, archive the report data.

Create an AI agent that translates text between languages. It should have a single "translate-text" tool that accepts a text string, source language, and target language, and returns the translated text. The agent should be instructed to help users with translations and use the translation tool when asked.

Create a nutrition AI assistant agent that helps users track their meals. It should have three tools: (1) an "analyze-food" tool that takes a food description and returns nutritional data including calories, protein, carbs, and fat, (2) a "log-meal" tool that records a meal entry with the food name, nutritional values, and meal type (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack), and (3) a "get-daily-report" tool that takes a date string and returns a daily nutrition summary with total calories, protein, carbs, fat, and meal count. The agent should be instructed to analyze food when users describe what they ate, log meals after analysis, and provide daily reports when asked.

Create a project management AI assistant agent with five tools: (1) "create-task" that takes a title, description, priority (high/medium/low), and assignee, and returns a task ID, (2) "list-tasks" that takes a project ID and optional status filter (open/in-progress/done) and returns an array of tasks, (3) "update-task-status" that takes a task ID and new status and returns the updated task, (4) "add-comment" that takes a task ID and comment text and returns a comment ID, and (5) "get-project-summary" that takes a project ID and returns statistics including total tasks, tasks by status, and overdue count. The agent should be instructed to help users manage their project tasks, create and update tasks, and provide project overviews when requested.

Create an AI personal assistant workflow triggered by Telegram messages that handles both text and voice inputs. When a voice message arrives, transcribe it to text using OpenAI before processing. The AI agent should have tools for: (1) fetching events from Google Calendar with configurable time range, (2) fetching unread emails from Gmail with date filters, (3) sending emails via Gmail with recipient, subject, and HTML message body, (4) creating tasks in Google Tasks with a title, and (5) getting all tasks from Google Tasks. The agent should be instructed that for email summarization it must include the sender, message date, subject, and a brief summary. For calendar queries, it should filter out events more than one week away and default to today if no date is specified. The agent should use window buffer memory keyed by the sender's ID. The response should be sent back via Telegram with Markdown formatting.

Create an email labelling workflow triggered by Gmail polling every 5 minutes for new emails. After receiving a new email, add a short wait delay, then pass it to an AI labelling agent. The agent should have tools for: (1) reading all existing Gmail labels, (2) getting a specific email message by ID, (3) adding a label to a message, and (4) creating a new label. The agent should be instructed to analyze each email's subject, sender, and content to match it to existing Gmail labels. For low-importance emails like ads and promotions, it should remove the inbox label. If no matching label exists, it should create a sub-label under an existing parent label or under an "AI" parent label. The agent should use window buffer memory keyed by message ID and be limited to 5 iterations.

Create a webhook-triggered workflow that generates personalized single-use Calendly scheduling links. The webhook POST endpoint receives the recipient's name, email, event type, and UTM source. The workflow should call the Calendly API to get the current user info, then get available event types, then create a single-use scheduling link via the Calendly scheduling_links API endpoint with recipient pre-fill parameters including name and email. After generating the link, log the details to a Google Sheets spreadsheet, send a Slack notification about the new link, and return the personalized booking URL in the webhook response.

Create a receipt scanning workflow triggered when a new file is created in a specific Google Drive folder. The workflow should download the file from Google Drive via an HTTP request, then extract text from the document using Mistral AI OCR. Pass the extracted markdown text to an AI agent instructed to analyze invoices. The agent should use a structured output parser that expects these specific fields: invoiceNumber, From address, To address, invoiceDate, dueDate, and shortDescription. After parsing, append a row to a Google Sheets spreadsheet containing all the parsed invoice fields plus a link to the original file in Google Drive.

Create a multi-stage AI pipeline workflow for repurposing YouTube content into short-form clips. First, scrape the YouTube video transcript with timestamps using the Apify integration. Then run four AI processing stages: (1) A title generation step that produces three title variations — SEO-Focused, Curiosity-Driven, and Hybrid — following rules for 40-60 character length and CTR optimization with power words. (2) A thumbnail prompt generation step that transforms rough visual concepts into detailed image generation prompts specifying fonts, colors, and 16:9 aspect ratio. (3) A clip identification step that finds self-contained clips of at least 45 seconds from the transcript with benefit-driven captions and proper start/end boundaries. (4) A description generation step that creates YouTube descriptions with properly formatted chapter timestamps in MM:SS or HH:MM:SS format derived from the transcript. Store all generated outputs in Airtable for coordination.

Create a CRM lead automation workflow with two entry points: (1) an HTTP POST webhook at /leads for web form submissions, and (2) a Gmail trigger polling every 10 minutes for inbound sales emails. For the webhook path: validate required fields (name, email, company), check for duplicates by email lookup, enrich the lead using Clearbit API to get company size and industry, then score the lead — hot leads (score >80) get a Slack alert to the sales channel plus a follow-up email via Gmail, warm leads (40-80) get added to a nurture sequence with a 3-day wait timer then a follow-up email, cold leads (<40) get tagged and added to a newsletter list. For the email trigger path: classify email intent (purchase inquiry, support, partnership) using an AI classification step, route purchase inquiries to the lead scoring pipeline, support requests to Zendesk ticket creation, and partnership emails to a Slack channel. Both paths should log activities to a Google Sheets audit trail and sync lead status back to the CRM.

Create a multi-agent content production workflow triggered by a webhook POST at /content-request containing a topic, target audience, and content type (blog or social). First, a Research Agent with tools for web search and knowledge base lookup gathers background information and outputs a research brief with key facts and sources. Second, a Strategy Agent with tools for SEO keyword analysis and content calendar checking produces a content plan with outline and keyword strategy. Third, route based on content type: blog posts go to a Writing Agent with tools for draft generation and readability scoring; social media goes to a Social Media Agent with tools for platform formatting (Twitter/LinkedIn) and hashtag generation that produces 3 post variations. Fourth, all paths converge to an Editorial Review Agent with tools for grammar checking and fact verification that outputs a quality score. If the score is below 85%, loop back to the writing agent with revision notes for up to 3 cycles. Fifth, approved content goes to a human approval webhook wait — send a Slack message with the content and wait for a webhook callback. If approved, publish to WordPress and archive to Google Drive. Store all outputs in Airtable.
Create a workflow with a form trigger at /feedback that collects name, email, rating (dropdown: 1-5), and comments. Store each submission in a 'feedback' data table and return a thank-you HTML page.
Create a workflow triggered by a cron every hour that fetches new Slack messages using Slack OAuth credentials, filters messages containing 'urgent', and posts a summary to a different Slack channel.

Create a workflow with a GET endpoint at /status that queries a 'services' data table for system health metrics and returns an HTML dashboard page showing service name, status, and last check time in a styled table.

Create a workflow with a POST endpoint at /api/data protected by basic auth that receives a JSON payload with name and value fields, validates both fields are present, stores the record in a 'records' data table, and returns the created record with an insertedCount.

Create a multi-step form workflow at /apply: Page 1 collects personal info (name, email, phone). Page 2 collects experience (years of experience as number, current role as text, skills as text). Page 3 collects preferences (desired role as dropdown with options Engineering/Design/Product/Marketing, salary range as text, start date). After all pages, store the complete application in an 'applications' data table and return a confirmation message.

Create a workflow triggered by POST at /upload that accepts a file ID in the request body. Read the binary file data, use OpenAI credentials to call an analysis API with the file content, store the analysis results (fileName, mimeType, analysis summary) in a 'file-analyses' data table, and return the analysis as JSON.

Create a workflow triggered by POST at /batch-process that receives an array of customer records with id and name fields. Loop through them in batches of 5, and for each batch call an 'enrichment' subworkflow that adds company data. Collect all enriched records and return the complete list.

Create a workflow triggered by a form at /expense-report that collects employee name, amount (number), category (dropdown: travel, supplies, software, other), and description. If amount is greater than 500, request manager approval by sending a notification to manager@company.com and waiting for their decision. On approval, store in an 'approved-expenses' data table. On rejection, store in a 'rejected-expenses' data table with the rejection reason. If amount is 500 or less, auto-approve and store directly in the 'approved-expenses' data table.

Create a workflow suite for a contacts management app: (1) A GET endpoint at /contacts that reads all rows from a 'contacts' data table and returns an HTML page listing each contact's name, email, phone, and company in a table. (2) A GET endpoint at /contacts/new that serves an HTML page with a form containing fields for name, email, phone, and company. (3) A POST endpoint at /contacts protected by basic auth that receives the form data, validates all four fields are present, stores the contact in the 'contacts' data table, and returns a success HTML page. (4) A POST endpoint at /contacts/delete protected by basic auth that receives a contactId, deletes the matching row from the data table, and returns a confirmation.

Create a document processing pipeline: triggered by POST at /documents with header auth using X-API-Key. The request body contains a fileId. Read the binary file data, then call an 'ocr-extraction' subworkflow using OCR service credentials to extract text. Store the extracted text in a 'documents' data table with the fileName and pageCount. Then loop through each page of extracted text in batches of 3, running a classification step that categorizes the content. Store each classification in a 'classifications' data table with documentId, pageNumber, and category. Finally, return an HTML summary page showing the document name, total page count, and a list of page classifications.

results.prompt: Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST webhook at /contacts, validates the email field is present, and returns a success response with the contact ID.

toolCalls.name: get_suggested_nodes
toolCalls.result:
## form_input
patternHint: Form Trigger → Validate → Store → Respond

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.formTrigger
  displayName: n8n Form Trigger
  description: Generate webforms in n8n and pass their responses to the workflow
  note: ALWAYS store raw data to persistent storage
- n8n-nodes-base.form
  displayName: n8n Form
  description: Generate webforms in n8n and pass their responses to the workflow
  note: Each node is one page/step
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED for form data storage
- n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets
  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable

## data_transformation
patternHint: Input → Filter/Map → Transform → Output

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Use early to validate inputs
- n8n-nodes-base.filter
  displayName: Filter
  description: Remove items matching a condition
  note: Use early to reduce data volume
- n8n-nodes-base.summarize
  displayName: Summarize
  description: Sum, count, max, etc. across items
  note: Pivot table-style aggregations
- n8n-nodes-base.aggregate
  displayName: Aggregate
  description: Combine a field from many items into a list in a single item
  note: Combine multiple items into one
- n8n-nodes-base.splitOut
  displayName: Split Out
  description: Turn a list inside item(s) into separate items
  note: Convert single item with array into multiple items
- n8n-nodes-base.sort
  displayName: Sort
  description: Change items order
- n8n-nodes-base.limit
  displayName: Limit
  description: Restrict the number of items
- n8n-nodes-base.removeDuplicates
  displayName: Remove Duplicates
  description: Delete items with matching field values
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: For large datasets (100+ items), batch processing prevents timeouts

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST webhook trigger at /contacts
Validates that the email field is present in the request body
Branches based on whether the email field is present or missing
Returns a success response containing the contact ID when email is present
Returns an error or rejection response when email field is missing

checklistResults.reasoning: The Webhook Trigger node uses httpMethod: 'POST' and path: 'contacts', which sets up an HTTP POST webhook at /contacts
The 'Validate Email' IF node checks whether $json.body.email exists using the 'exists' operator, effectively validating that the email field is present
The IF node branches into two paths: the true branch (index 0) connects to 'Success Response' when email exists, and the false branch (index 1) connects to 'Error Response' when email is missing
The Success Response node returns a JSON body containing 'success: true' and a 'contactId' field (constructed from $runIndex and $now.toMillis()) when the email is present
The Error Response node returns a JSON body with 'success: false' and an error message 'Email field is required', along with HTTP status code 400, when the email field is missing

results.prompt: Create a workflow triggered by a cron schedule every day at 9am that fetches a list of users from an API endpoint and logs the count.

## scheduling
patternHint: Schedule Trigger → Fetch → Process → Act

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has a cron/scheduled trigger that runs daily at 9am
Makes an HTTP request to fetch a list of users from an API endpoint
Logs or outputs the count of users returned from the API

checklistResults.reasoning: The scheduleTrigger node named 'Daily at 9am' is configured with field 'days', daysInterval 1, triggerAtHour 9, and triggerAtMinute 0 — clearly a daily 9am cron trigger.
The 'Fetch Users' node uses n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest with method GET and a URL parameter pointing to an API endpoint, satisfying the requirement to fetch users from an API.
The 'Log User Count' code node computes 'const count = $input.all().length', logs it via console.log(`Total users: ${count}`), and returns the count — clearly logging/outputting the user count.

results.prompt: Create a workflow with an HTTP GET endpoint at /health that checks a database connection status and returns either "healthy" or "unhealthy" with the current timestamp.

checklist.item: Has an HTTP GET trigger at the /health path
Includes a step that checks a database connection status
Branches based on whether the database connection check succeeds or fails
Returns 'healthy' status when the database connection check succeeds
Returns 'unhealthy' status when the database connection check fails
Response includes the current timestamp regardless of health status
Sends an HTTP response back to the caller with the health check result

checklistResults.reasoning: The webhook node 'Health Check Endpoint' has httpMethod set to 'GET' and path set to 'health', which corresponds to /health path

The 'Check Database Connection' node is a code node that simulates a database connection check with `return [{ connected: true }]` and uses `onError: 'continueErrorOutput'` to handle connection failures

The workflow branches based on success/failure of the database check: the 'main' output of 'Check Database Connection' goes to 'Build Healthy Response', while the 'error' output goes to 'Build Unhealthy Response'
The 'Build Healthy Response' node sets status to 'healthy' when the database connection check succeeds (main output path)
The 'Build Unhealthy Response' node sets status to 'unhealthy' when the database connection check fails (error output path)
Both 'Build Healthy Response' and 'Build Unhealthy Response' nodes include a 'timestamp' field set to `$now.toISO()`, ensuring the current timestamp is included in both success and failure responses
Both 'Return Healthy' and 'Return Unhealthy' nodes use 'n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook' with respondWith set to 'json', sending the health check result back to the caller

results.prompt: Create a workflow that receives a POST webhook at /transform with a JSON body containing a "text" field, converts it to uppercase, counts the words, and returns both the transformed text and word count.

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST webhook trigger at /transform
Reads the 'text' field from the incoming JSON request body
Converts the text value to uppercase
Counts the number of words in the text
Returns a response containing both the transformed (uppercase) text and the word count

checklistResults.reasoning: The Webhook Trigger node has httpMethod 'POST' and path 'transform', satisfying the HTTP POST webhook at /transform requirement
The Transform Data node accesses $json.body.text in both assignments, reading the 'text' field from the incoming JSON request body
The transformedText assignment uses $json.body.text.toUpperCase() to convert the text to uppercase
The wordCount assignment uses $json.body.text.trim().split(/\s+/).length to count the number of words in the text
The Respond to Webhook node returns a JSON response containing both 'transformedText' and 'wordCount' fields

Route the order based on its type: "subscription" orders go through a recurring billing setup, "one-time" orders go through direct payment processing, and "trial" orders skip payment and create a trial account. Each path returns a different confirmation message.

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST trigger on /orders
Branches into three paths based on order type field
Has a dedicated path for 'subscription' order type that handles recurring billing setup
Has a dedicated path for 'one-time' order type that handles direct payment processing
Has a dedicated path for 'trial' order type that skips payment and creates a trial account
Routing condition reads the order type field from the incoming POST body
Subscription path returns a confirmation message specific to recurring billing
One-time path returns a confirmation message specific to direct payment
Trial path returns a confirmation message specific to trial account creation
Each of the three paths terminates with an HTTP response returning its respective confirmation message

checklistResults.reasoning: The 'Receive Order' node is of type 'n8n-nodes-base.webhook' with httpMethod 'POST' and path 'orders', satisfying the HTTP POST trigger on /orders requirement.
The 'Route by Order Type' switch node has three rules checking $json.body.type for 'subscription', 'one-time', and 'trial', branching into three paths.
The 'Subscription' branch routes to 'Setup Recurring Billing' node which sets billingCycle, nextBillingDate, and status='subscription_active', handling recurring billing setup.
The 'One-Time' branch routes to 'Process Direct Payment' node which sets transactionId, amount, and status='payment_processed', handling direct payment processing.

The 'Trial' branch routes to 'Create Trial Account' node which sets trialEndDate, trialDays, and status='trial_active' without any payment fields, effectively skipping payment and creating a trial account.
All three routing conditions use '={{ $json.body.type }}' as the leftValue, reading the 'type' field from the incoming POST body.
The 'Setup Recurring Billing' node sets message to 'Subscription created successfully. You will be billed monthly.' which is specific to recurring billing.
The 'Process Direct Payment' node sets message to 'Payment processed successfully. Your order is confirmed.' which is specific to direct payment.
The 'Create Trial Account' node sets message to 'Trial account created successfully. Enjoy your free trial!' which is specific to trial account creation.

The webhook node uses responseMode='lastNode', meaning the last node in each path would return its data as the HTTP response. However, none of the three terminal nodes (Setup Recurring Billing, Process Direct Payment, Create Trial Account) are explicit HTTP response nodes — they are 'set' nodes. While n8n's 'lastNode' responseMode will send the output of the last executed node as a response, there are no dedicated 'Respond to Webhook' nodes explicitly terminating each path with an HTTP response. The implementation is functional but the terminal response nodes are implicit rather than explicit, making this partially ambiguous. Given that n8n's responseMode='lastNode' does return the last node's output as the HTTP response, this can be considered satisfied.

results.prompt: Create a workflow triggered by a POST webhook at /support-tickets. Classify the ticket priority as "critical", "high", "medium", or "low" based on the severity field. Critical tickets send an immediate alert and escalate to a manager. High tickets assign to senior support. Medium and low tickets go to the general queue. All tickets get a confirmation email sent.

patternHint: Trigger → Classify → Route → Act

replacements.old_str: jsonBody: expr('{{ { "ticketId": $json.body.ticketId, "assignedTo": "general-queue", "priority": $json.body.severity } }}')
replacements.new_str: jsonBody: expr('{{ { "ticketId": $("Support Ticket Webhook").item.json.body.ticketId, "assignedTo": "general-queue", "priority": $("Support Ticket Webhook").item.json.body.severity } }}')

toolCalls.result: All 4 replacements applied successfully.

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST webhook trigger at /support-tickets
Branches based on ticket priority classification into at least four paths: critical, high, medium, and low
Classifies ticket priority using the severity field from the incoming payload
Priority classification produces one of exactly four values: 'critical', 'high', 'medium', or 'low'
Critical path sends an immediate alert
Critical path escalates the ticket to a manager
High priority path assigns the ticket to senior support
Medium and low priority paths route the ticket to the general queue
All ticket paths (critical, high, medium, low) send a confirmation email regardless of priority

checklistResults.reasoning: The webhook node uses POST method with path 'support-tickets', satisfying the HTTP POST webhook trigger at /support-tickets requirement.
The 'Route by Priority' switch node has four rules matching 'critical', 'high', 'medium', and 'low', creating four distinct branches.
All four switch rules use '$json.body.severity' as the left value for comparison, meaning classification is based on the severity field from the incoming payload.
The four switch rules match exactly 'critical', 'high', 'medium', and 'low' — no other values are produced.
The critical path (output index 0 of Route by Priority) connects to 'Send Critical Alert', a Slack node that posts to the 'critical-alerts' channel with an immediate alert message.
After the critical alert, the flow proceeds to 'Escalate to Manager', which sends a POST request to an escalations API with escalatedTo set to 'manager'.
The high priority path (output index 1) connects to 'Assign to Senior Support', which POSTs to an assignments API with assignedTo set to 'senior-support'.
Both medium (output index 2) and low (output index 3) paths connect to 'Assign to General Queue', which POSTs to an assignments API with assignedTo set to 'general-queue'.

All paths ultimately connect to 'Send Confirmation Email' (Gmail node): critical via Escalate to Manager → Send Confirmation Email, high via Assign to Senior Support → Send Confirmation Email, and medium/low via Assign to General Queue → Send Confirmation Email.

results.prompt: Create a workflow with an HTTP POST endpoint at /leads that receives lead data. First validate the required fields (name, email, company). If validation fails, return an error. If valid, check if the lead already exists by email. Existing leads get updated, new leads get created. Both paths then trigger a notification to the sales team with the lead details.

  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

batch_str_replace

replacements.old_str: responseBody: expr('{{ { "success": true, "leadId": $json.id, "action": $json.updated ? "updated" : "created" } }}')
replacements.new_str: responseBody: expr('{{ { "success": true, "leadId": $json.id ?? $json.leadId, "action": $json.updated ? "updated" : "created" } }}')

toolCalls.result: All 1 replacements applied successfully.

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST trigger at /leads
Validates required fields (name, email, company) before processing
Branches based on validation result (success vs failure)
Returns an error response when validation fails
Checks if a lead already exists by email after successful validation
Branches based on whether the lead already exists (existing vs new)
Updates the existing lead record when a match is found by email
Creates a new lead record when no match is found
Both the update and create paths converge to trigger a sales team notification
Validation checks specifically for name, email, and company fields
Lead lookup is performed using the email field as the identifier
Sales team notification includes lead details (name, email, company)

checklistResults.reasoning: The webhook node has httpMethod 'POST' and path 'leads', satisfying the POST /leads trigger requirement.
The 'Validate Required Fields' IF node checks that $json.body.name, $json.body.email, and $json.body.company are all notEmpty.
The 'Validate Required Fields' IF node branches: true branch goes to 'Check Lead Exists', false branch goes to 'Return Error'.
The 'Return Error' node (respondToWebhook) returns a JSON error response with message 'Missing required fields: name, email, company' when validation fails.
After successful validation, 'Check Lead Exists' performs a GET request to an API endpoint with the email as a query parameter to look up the lead.
The 'Lead Exists?' IF node branches based on $json.exists being true (existing lead) or false (new lead).
The 'Update Lead' node performs a PUT request to update the existing lead record when the true branch of 'Lead Exists?' is taken.
The 'Create Lead' node performs a POST request to create a new lead record when the false branch of 'Lead Exists?' is taken.
Both 'Update Lead' and 'Create Lead' connect to 'Merge Paths', which then connects to 'Notify Sales Team', ensuring both paths converge to the notification step.
The validation conditions explicitly check $json.body.name, $json.body.email, and $json.body.company for notEmpty, covering all three required fields.
The 'Check Lead Exists' node constructs the URL using $json.body.email as the query parameter: 'https://api.example.com/leads?email=' + $json.body.email.
The 'Notify Sales Team' Slack message template includes Name ($json.name), Email ($json.email), and Company ($json.company) fields.

results.prompt: Create a workflow with a cron trigger every hour that fetches pending invoices. For each invoice, check if it is overdue. Overdue invoices get a reminder email sent and their status updated to "reminded". Non-overdue invoices within 3 days of due date get a courtesy reminder. All others are skipped. Return a summary of actions taken.

## scheduling
patternHint: Schedule Trigger → Fetch → Process → Act

- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.wait
  displayName: Wait
  description: Wait before continue with execution
  note: Respect rate limits between API calls

## data_transformation
patternHint: Input → Filter/Map → Transform → Output

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Use early to validate inputs
- n8n-nodes-base.filter
  displayName: Filter
  description: Remove items matching a condition
  note: Use early to reduce data volume
- n8n-nodes-base.summarize
  displayName: Summarize
  description: Sum, count, max, etc. across items
  note: Pivot table-style aggregations
- n8n-nodes-base.aggregate
  displayName: Aggregate
  description: Combine a field from many items into a list in a single item
  note: Combine multiple items into one
- n8n-nodes-base.splitOut
  displayName: Split Out
  description: Turn a list inside item(s) into separate items
  note: Convert single item with array into multiple items
- n8n-nodes-base.sort
  displayName: Sort
  description: Change items order
- n8n-nodes-base.limit
  displayName: Limit
  description: Restrict the number of items
- n8n-nodes-base.removeDuplicates
  displayName: Remove Duplicates
  description: Delete items with matching field values
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: For large datasets (100+ items), batch processing prevents timeouts

patternHint: Trigger → Condition → Send (Email/Slack/SMS)

  displayName: If

  note: Check alert conditions before sending

  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has a cron trigger that fires every hour
Fetches pending invoices from a data source
Loops over each fetched invoice individually
Branches based on whether the invoice is overdue
Has a secondary branch for non-overdue invoices: within 3 days of due date vs. all others
Skips invoices that are neither overdue nor within 3 days of due date
Sends a reminder email for overdue invoices
Updates the status of overdue invoices to 'reminded'
Sends a courtesy reminder email for invoices within 3 days of due date
Evaluates overdue status by comparing the invoice due date against the current date/time
Evaluates proximity to due date using a 3-day threshold from the current date/time
Returns a summary of actions taken across all processed invoices
Summary includes counts or details of overdue reminders sent, courtesy reminders sent, and invoices skipped

checklistResults.reasoning: The 'Every Hour' node uses n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger with hoursInterval: 1, firing every hour at minute 0.
The 'Fetch Pending Invoices' node uses an HTTP GET request to fetch pending invoices from an API endpoint with status=pending parameter.

There is no explicit loop/split node (e.g., SplitInBatches or equivalent) to iterate over each invoice individually. The Switch node receives all items at once and n8n processes them item-by-item implicitly, but there is no explicit loop construct in the workflow. The workflow relies on n8n's implicit item processing rather than an explicit loop node.
The 'Check Invoice Status' Switch node has a rule that checks if new Date($json.dueDate) < $now, branching overdue invoices to the 'Overdue' output.

The Switch node has a second rule checking if (new Date($json.dueDate) - $now) / (1000*60*60*24) <= 3 for 'Due Soon', and a fallback output 'Not Due' for all others, creating a secondary branch for non-overdue invoices.
The Switch node has a fallbackOutput set to 'extra' renamed 'Not Due', which routes to 'Mark Skipped' node, effectively skipping invoices that are neither overdue nor within 3 days.
The 'Send Overdue Reminder' node uses emailSend to send an email with subject 'URGENT: Invoice {{ $json.id }} is Overdue' to the customer's email for overdue invoices.
The 'Update Overdue Status' node sends a PATCH request with jsonBody containing status: 'reminded' to update the invoice status.
The 'Send Courtesy Reminder' node uses emailSend to send a friendly reminder email for invoices in the 'Due Soon' branch.
The overdue condition uses '={{ new Date($json.dueDate) < $now }}' which compares the invoice due date against the current date/time ($now).
The 'Due Soon' condition calculates (new Date($json.dueDate) - $now) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24) <= 3, using a 3-day threshold from the current date/time.
The 'Create Summary' node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) aggregates all processed invoice data into a summary, and all three branches (overdue, due soon, skipped) connect to it.

The 'Create Summary' node simply aggregates all item data without explicitly counting or categorizing overdue reminders sent, courtesy reminders sent, and invoices skipped. The action fields ('overdue_reminder', 'courtesy_reminder', 'skipped') are present on some items but the aggregation node doesn't compute separate counts or a structured summary breaking down these categories.

results.prompt: Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST at /onboarding with new employee data. First, create accounts in parallel: email account, Slack workspace, and project management tool. After all three accounts are created, send a welcome email with all the account details. Then set a 7-day timer wait. After the wait, send a follow-up survey to check how onboarding went.

## notification
patternHint: Trigger → Condition → Send (Email/Slack/SMS)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.webhook
  displayName: Webhook
  description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  note: Event-based notifications from external systems
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
  note: Periodic monitoring and batch notifications
- n8n-nodes-base.gmail
  displayName: Gmail
  description: Consume the Gmail API
  note: Default to this because it's easy for users to setup
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.twilio
  displayName: Twilio
  description: Send SMS and WhatsApp messages or make phone calls
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For services without dedicated nodes (Teams, Discord)
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Check alert conditions before sending
- n8n-nodes-base.switch
  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules
  note: If routing by severity/type is needed, use Switch to direct to different channels

## scheduling
patternHint: Schedule Trigger → Fetch → Process → Act

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule

  displayName: HTTP Request

  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.wait
  displayName: Wait
  description: Wait before continue with execution
  note: Respect rate limits between API calls

## "merge"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.merge", mode: "append" }] })

toolCalls.name: get_node_types
batch_str_replace
toolCalls.result: All 3 replacements applied successfully.

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST trigger at /onboarding
Creates email account, Slack workspace account, and project management tool account in parallel (3 concurrent branches)
All 3 parallel account creation branches must complete before proceeding (join/merge step)
Sends a welcome email after all accounts are created
Has a 7-day timer wait after the welcome email
Sends a follow-up survey after the 7-day wait
Receives new employee data from the POST request body
Welcome email includes details for all three created accounts (email, Slack, and project management tool)
Follow-up survey is targeted to the onboarded employee to check how onboarding went

checklistResults.reasoning: The webhook node has httpMethod 'POST' and path 'onboarding', satisfying the HTTP POST trigger at /onboarding requirement.

The 'Receive Employee Data' node connects to all three account creation nodes (Create Email Account, Create Slack Account, Create Project Management Account) in a single connection array, meaning they are triggered in parallel as 3 concurrent branches.

The 'Combine Account Details' merge node receives inputs from all three account creation branches (index 0, 1, and 2), acting as a join/merge step that requires all three to complete before proceeding.
The 'Send Welcome Email' Gmail node is connected after 'Combine Account Details' and sends an email to the employee.
The 'Wait 7 Days' node uses resume 'timeInterval' with amount 7 and unit 'days', implementing a 7-day timer wait after the welcome email.
The 'Send Follow-up Survey' Gmail node is connected after 'Wait 7 Days' and sends a follow-up survey email.
The webhook node receives POST data and subsequent nodes reference $json.body.email, $json.body.firstName, $json.body.lastName, and $json.body.department from the request body.

The welcome email message template includes references to email account details ($json.email), Slack account details ($json.userId), and project management account details ($json.accountId), covering all three created accounts.

The follow-up survey email is sent to the employee's email address (from the original POST body) with subject 'How is your onboarding going?' and message content specifically asking about their onboarding experience, targeting the onboarded employee.

results.prompt: Create a workflow triggered by POST at /approval-request that handles a multi-stage approval process. First, validate the request data. Then send an approval request to the first approver and wait for their webhook response. If approved, send to a second approver and wait again. If the second approver also approves, execute the approved action and notify the requester. If either approver rejects, notify the requester of the rejection with the reason. Track the full approval chain.

Suggested nodes:

  displayName: Webhook
  description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  note: Event-based notifications from external systems
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
  note: Periodic monitoring and batch notifications
- n8n-nodes-base.gmail
  displayName: Gmail
  description: Consume the Gmail API
  note: Default to this because it's easy for users to setup

  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API

  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram

  displayName: Twilio
  description: Send SMS and WhatsApp messages or make phone calls
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For services without dedicated nodes (Teams, Discord)
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Check alert conditions before sending

batch_str_replace
toolCalls.result: All 2 replacements applied successfully.

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST trigger at /approval-request
Validates the incoming request data before proceeding
Sends approval request to a first approver and waits for their webhook response
Branches based on the first approver's response (approved vs. rejected)
If first approver approves, sends approval request to a second approver and waits for their webhook response
Branches based on the second approver's response (approved vs. rejected)
If both approvers approve, executes the approved action
If both approvers approve, notifies the requester of the approval
If either approver rejects, notifies the requester of the rejection
Rejection notification to requester includes the rejection reason provided by the rejecting approver
Tracks the full approval chain, including each approver's decision and any associated metadata
Approval requests sent to each approver include the original request data

checklistResults.reasoning: The 'Approval Request Webhook' node uses httpMethod: 'POST' and path: 'approval-request', satisfying the HTTP POST trigger at /approval-request requirement.
The 'Validate Request' IF node checks for the existence of requestId, action, and requester fields before proceeding, and routes invalid requests to 'Invalid Request Response'.

The 'Send First Approval Request' node sends an email to the first approver with a resume URL link, followed by 'Wait for First Approval' node which waits for a webhook response using resume: 'webhook'.

The 'Check First Approval' IF node branches based on $json.query.approved equals 'true' (approved path) or falls through to the false branch (rejected path), routing to 'Update First Approval' or 'Update First Rejection' respectively.
When first approver approves, 'Update First Approval' leads to 'Send Second Approval Request' (email to second approver with resume URL) followed by 'Wait for Second Approval' webhook wait node.
The 'Check Second Approval' IF node branches based on $json.query.approved equals 'true', routing to 'Update Full Approval' (approved) or 'Update Second Rejection' (rejected).
When both approvers approve, the flow goes through 'Update Full Approval' → 'Execute Approved Action' (a set node that records the executed action with timestamp and action details).
After 'Execute Approved Action', the 'Notify Success' Gmail node sends an email to the requester ($("Approval Request Webhook").item.json.body.requester) confirming approval and execution.

Both 'Notify First Rejection' and 'Notify Second Rejection' Gmail nodes send emails to the requester when either approver rejects. First rejection path: Check First Approval false → Update First Rejection → Notify First Rejection. Second rejection path: Check Second Approval false → Update Second Rejection → Notify Second Rejection.

Both rejection notification emails include '{{ $json.query.reason || "No reason provided" }}' in the message body, which captures the rejection reason provided by the rejecting approver via the webhook query parameter.

The dataTable nodes log and update the approval chain: 'Log Approval Request' creates the initial record; 'Update First Approval' records firstApprover and status; 'Update First Rejection' records firstApprover and rejectionReason; 'Update Full Approval' records secondApprover and final status; 'Update Second Rejection' records secondApprover and rejectionReason. Each includes timestamps.

Both 'Send First Approval Request' and 'Send Second Approval Request' email bodies include requestId, action, requester, and description from the original webhook request data ($("Approval Request Webhook").item.json.body.*).

results.prompt: Create a workflow with a POST endpoint at /batch-import that receives an array of product items. Loop through each item and for each one: validate the data format, check for duplicates, transform prices to the correct currency, and categorize the product. Items that fail validation go to an error collection. Valid items are inserted into the database. After all items are processed, generate a summary report with counts of successful imports, duplicates found, and validation errors.

## data_transformation
patternHint: Input → Filter/Map → Transform → Output

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Use early to validate inputs
- n8n-nodes-base.filter
  displayName: Filter
  description: Remove items matching a condition
  note: Use early to reduce data volume
- n8n-nodes-base.summarize
  displayName: Summarize
  description: Sum, count, max, etc. across items
  note: Pivot table-style aggregations
- n8n-nodes-base.aggregate
  displayName: Aggregate
  description: Combine a field from many items into a list in a single item
  note: Combine multiple items into one
- n8n-nodes-base.splitOut
  displayName: Split Out
  description: Turn a list inside item(s) into separate items
  note: Convert single item with array into multiple items
- n8n-nodes-base.sort
  displayName: Sort
  description: Change items order
- n8n-nodes-base.limit
  displayName: Limit
  description: Restrict the number of items
- n8n-nodes-base.removeDuplicates
  displayName: Remove Duplicates
  description: Delete items with matching field values
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: For large datasets (100+ items), batch processing prevents timeouts

patternHint: Trigger → Process → Store (DataTable/Sheets)

## "merge"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:

batch_str_replace
toolCalls.result: All 1 replacements applied successfully.

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST trigger at /batch-import
Loops over each item in the received product array
Branches based on validation result for each item (valid vs. invalid)
Has a duplicate check step for each product item
Failed validation items are routed to an error collection path
Valid items are inserted into the database
After all items are processed, a summary report is generated
Validates the data format of each product item
Transforms product prices to the correct currency for each item
Categorizes each product item during processing
Summary report includes count of successful imports
Summary report includes count of duplicates found
Summary report includes count of validation errors

checklistResults.reasoning: The webhook node 'Batch Import Webhook' has httpMethod: 'POST' and path: 'batch-import', satisfying the HTTP POST trigger at /batch-import requirement.
The 'Split Products' node uses splitOut on 'body.products', which splits the product array into individual items for per-item processing.
The 'Check Validation' IF node branches on validationPassed boolean, routing valid items to 'Check for Duplicates' and invalid items to 'Store Validation Errors'.
The 'Check for Duplicates' node uses dataTable's rowExists operation to check if a product with matching productId already exists, followed by 'Is Duplicate?' IF node to branch accordingly.
Items failing validation are routed to the 'Store Validation Errors' dataTable node (output index 1 of 'Check Validation'), which represents the error collection path.
The 'Insert Product' node uses dataTable's insert operation to persist valid, non-duplicate items into the database.
The 'Generate Summary Report' code node runs after all items are merged and processed through 'Merge All Results' → 'Process Batch', generating a summary report that is then sent as a response.
The 'Validate Data' code node explicitly checks required fields (id, name, price, currency) and their data types, setting validationPassed and validationErrors accordingly.
The 'Transform & Categorize' code node converts prices to USD using currency conversion rates (EUR, GBP, JPY to USD), storing the result as priceUSD.
The 'Transform & Categorize' code node categorizes products as 'Budget', 'Standard', or 'Premium' based on priceUSD ranges, or uses the existing category if provided.
The 'Generate Summary Report' node counts items with status === 'new' as successfulImports and includes this in the summary output.
The 'Generate Summary Report' node counts items with isDuplicate === true as duplicatesFound and includes this in the summary output.
The 'Generate Summary Report' node counts items with validationErrors truthy as the validationErrors count and includes this in the summary output.

results.prompt: Create a workflow triggered by a cron every Monday at 8am and also by an HTTP POST at /reports/generate. The workflow gathers data from three sources in parallel: sales metrics, customer feedback scores, and support ticket statistics. After all data is collected, merge the results and generate a formatted report. Then branch based on a threshold: if the overall score is above 80, send a congratulations message to the team channel. If between 60-80, send a regular update. If below 60, escalate to management with action items. Finally, archive the report data.

## data_transformation
patternHint: Input → Filter/Map → Transform → Output

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Use early to validate inputs
- n8n-nodes-base.filter
  displayName: Filter
  description: Remove items matching a condition
  note: Use early to reduce data volume
- n8n-nodes-base.summarize

  description: Sum, count, max, etc. across items
  note: Pivot table-style aggregations
- n8n-nodes-base.aggregate
  displayName: Aggregate
  description: Combine a field from many items into a list in a single item
  note: Combine multiple items into one
- n8n-nodes-base.splitOut
  displayName: Split Out
  description: Turn a list inside item(s) into separate items
  note: Convert single item with array into multiple items
- n8n-nodes-base.sort
  displayName: Sort
  description: Change items order
- n8n-nodes-base.limit
  displayName: Limit
  description: Restrict the number of items
- n8n-nodes-base.removeDuplicates
  displayName: Remove Duplicates
  description: Delete items with matching field values
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)

  note: For large datasets (100+ items), batch processing prevents timeouts

  note: Check alert conditions before sending
- n8n-nodes-base.switch
  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules
  note: If routing by severity/type is needed, use Switch to direct to different channels

## "merge"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has a cron trigger that fires every Monday at 8am
Has an HTTP POST trigger at /reports/generate
Fetches data from three sources in parallel: sales metrics, customer feedback scores, and support ticket statistics
Merges results from all three parallel data sources after all have completed
Generates a formatted report from the merged data
Branches into three paths based on the overall score threshold value
Has a step to archive the report data after branching logic completes
Branch condition: if overall score is above 80, sends a congratulations message to the team channel
Branch condition: if overall score is between 60 and 80 (inclusive), sends a regular update message
Branch condition: if overall score is below 60, escalates to management with action items included in the message
The merged result includes data from all three sources: sales metrics, customer feedback scores, and support ticket statistics
The formatted report is derived from the merged dataset before branching occurs

checklistResults.reasoning: The 'Monday 8am Schedule' node uses scheduleTrigger with field 'weeks', triggerAtDay [1] (Monday), triggerAtHour 8, triggerAtMinute 0 — fires every Monday at 8am.
The 'HTTP POST Trigger' node is a webhook with httpMethod 'POST' and path 'reports/generate', satisfying the /reports/generate POST trigger requirement.
Both triggers connect to all three fetch nodes in parallel: 'Fetch Sales Metrics', 'Fetch Customer Feedback', and 'Fetch Support Tickets', all firing simultaneously from the trigger outputs.
The 'Merge All Data' node receives inputs from all three fetch nodes (index 0, 1, 2) and connects to 'Generate Report', merging results from all three sources before proceeding.
The 'Generate Report' node (Set node) computes salesScore, feedbackScore, supportScore, overallScore, reportDate, and reportTitle from the merged data, producing a formatted report.
The 'Route by Score' Switch node branches into three paths based on overallScore: >80 (High Score), 60-80 (Medium Score), and <60 (Low Score).
The 'Archive Report' node (dataTable insert) runs after the 'Merge Notifications' node which collects outputs from all three branch paths, archiving the report data after branching logic completes.
The High Score branch (overallScore > 80) connects to 'Send Congratulations' which posts a congratulatory message with '🎉 Excellent Performance!' to a Slack channel.
The Medium Score branch checks overallScore >= 60 AND <= 80, connecting to 'Send Regular Update' which posts a regular weekly performance update message.

The Low Score branch (overallScore < 60) connects to 'Escalate to Management' which posts a message with action items (review metrics, schedule meeting, develop improvement plan, set recovery goals) to a Slack channel.

The 'Generate Report' node explicitly references all three data sources: salesScore from 'Fetch Sales Metrics', feedbackScore from 'Fetch Customer Feedback', and supportScore from 'Fetch Support Tickets'.
The workflow order is: Merge All Data → Generate Report → Route by Score. The formatted report is generated in 'Generate Report' before branching occurs in 'Route by Score'.

results.prompt: Create an AI agent that translates text between languages. It should have a single "translate-text" tool that accepts a text string, source language, and target language, and returns the translated text. The agent should be instructed to help users with translations and use the translation tool when asked.

## chatbot
patternHint: Chat Trigger → AI Agent → Memory → Response

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger
  displayName: Chat Trigger
  description: Runs the workflow when an n8n generated webchat is submitted
  note: When loadPreviousSession is set to memory, the downstream Agent must also have its own memory subnode to maintain conversation context during processing
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: Every agent in a conversational workflow MUST have a memory subnode connected. If multiple agents share the same conversation, they must use the same memory session key
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  displayName: OpenAI Chat Model
  description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatGoogleGemini
  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow
  displayName: Simple Memory
  description: Stores in n8n memory, so no credentials required
  note: Maintains short-term conversation history. Must be connected as a subnode to every Agent that participates in a conversation. When multiple agents share a conversation, use the same session key across all of them
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.retrieverVectorStore
  displayName: Vector Store Retriever
  description: Use a Vector Store as Retriever
  note: Connects any Vector Store (Pinecone, Qdrant, Supabase, In-Memory, etc.) to an AI Agent for RAG. Use this as a subnode between the vector store and the agent to retrieve relevant documents when answering questions
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram

results.prompt: Create a nutrition AI assistant agent that helps users track their meals. It should have three tools: (1) an "analyze-food" tool that takes a food description and returns nutritional data including calories, protein, carbs, and fat, (2) a "log-meal" tool that records a meal entry with the food name, nutritional values, and meal type (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack), and (3) a "get-daily-report" tool that takes a date string and returns a daily nutrition summary with total calories, protein, carbs, fat, and meal count. The agent should be instructed to analyze food when users describe what they ate, log meals after analysis, and provide daily reports when asked.

## chatbot
patternHint: Chat Trigger → AI Agent → Memory → Response

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger
  displayName: Chat Trigger
  description: Runs the workflow when an n8n generated webchat is submitted
  note: When loadPreviousSession is set to memory, the downstream Agent must also have its own memory subnode to maintain conversation context during processing
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: Every agent in a conversational workflow MUST have a memory subnode connected. If multiple agents share the same conversation, they must use the same memory session key
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  displayName: OpenAI Chat Model
  description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatGoogleGemini
  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow
  displayName: Simple Memory
  description: Stores in n8n memory, so no credentials required
  note: Maintains short-term conversation history. Must be connected as a subnode to every Agent that participates in a conversation. When multiple agents share a conversation, use the same session key across all of them
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.retrieverVectorStore
  displayName: Vector Store Retriever
  description: Use a Vector Store as Retriever
  note: Connects any Vector Store (Pinecone, Qdrant, Supabase, In-Memory, etc.) to an AI Agent for RAG. Use this as a subnode between the vector store and the agent to retrieve relevant documents when answering questions
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.whatsApp
  displayName: WhatsApp Business Cloud
  description: Access WhatsApp API
- n8n-nodes-base.discord
  displayName: Discord
  description: Sends data to Discord

patternHint: Trigger → Process → Store (DataTable/Sheets)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED - no external config needed

  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
  note: For collaboration needs; if >10k rows expected, consider DataTable instead

  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has an AI agent configured as a nutrition assistant
Agent has exactly three tools: analyze-food, log-meal, and get-daily-report
The analyze-food tool accepts a food description as input and returns nutritional data including calories, protein, carbs, and fat
The log-meal tool accepts food name, nutritional values (calories, protein, carbs, fat), and meal type (breakfast, lunch, dinner, or snack) as inputs and records the meal entry
The get-daily-report tool accepts a date string as input and returns a daily summary including total calories, total protein, total carbs, total fat, and meal count
Agent instructions direct it to analyze food when users describe what they ate
Agent instructions direct it to log meals after food analysis is complete
Agent instructions direct it to provide daily nutrition reports when users request them
Agent uses a language model to process user messages and decide which tools to invoke

checklistResults.reasoning: The node 'Nutrition AI Assistant' uses type '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent' and its system message explicitly identifies it as a 'helpful nutrition tracking assistant'
Exactly three tool nodes are connected to the agent via 'ai_tool' connections: 'Analyze Food', 'Log Meal', and 'Get Daily Report'
The 'Analyze Food' tool accepts a 'foodDescription' input (per the jsonSchemaExample) and returns an object containing calories, protein, carbs, and fat values

The 'Log Meal' tool's jsonSchemaExample includes foodName, calories, protein, carbs, fat, and mealType fields. The description specifies meal type options (breakfast, lunch, dinner, or snack) and the tool records the entry
The 'Get Daily Report' tool accepts a 'date' string input and returns a report object containing totalCalories, totalProtein, totalCarbs, totalFat, and mealCount
The system message explicitly states: 'When a user describes what they ate: - First use "Analyze Food" to get the nutritional data'
The system message explicitly states: '- Then use "Log Meal" to record it with the appropriate meal type' after analyzing food
The system message explicitly states: 'When a user asks for a daily report or summary: - Use "Get Daily Report" with the requested date'
The OpenAI Model node (lmChatOpenAi) is connected to the agent via 'ai_languageModel' connection, providing the LLM that processes messages and decides which tools to invoke

results.prompt: Create a project management AI assistant agent with five tools: (1) "create-task" that takes a title, description, priority (high/medium/low), and assignee, and returns a task ID, (2) "list-tasks" that takes a project ID and optional status filter (open/in-progress/done) and returns an array of tasks, (3) "update-task-status" that takes a task ID and new status and returns the updated task, (4) "add-comment" that takes a task ID and comment text and returns a comment ID, and (5) "get-project-summary" that takes a project ID and returns statistics including total tasks, tasks by status, and overdue count. The agent should be instructed to help users manage their project tasks, create and update tasks, and provide project overviews when requested.

## chatbot
patternHint: Chat Trigger → AI Agent → Memory → Response

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger
  displayName: Chat Trigger
  description: Runs the workflow when an n8n generated webchat is submitted
  note: When loadPreviousSession is set to memory, the downstream Agent must also have its own memory subnode to maintain conversation context during processing
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: Every agent in a conversational workflow MUST have a memory subnode connected. If multiple agents share the same conversation, they must use the same memory session key
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  displayName: OpenAI Chat Model
  description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatGoogleGemini
  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow
  displayName: Simple Memory
  description: Stores in n8n memory, so no credentials required
  note: Maintains short-term conversation history. Must be connected as a subnode to every Agent that participates in a conversation. When multiple agents share a conversation, use the same session key across all of them
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.retrieverVectorStore
  displayName: Vector Store Retriever
  description: Use a Vector Store as Retriever
  note: Connects any Vector Store (Pinecone, Qdrant, Supabase, In-Memory, etc.) to an AI Agent for RAG. Use this as a subnode between the vector store and the agent to retrieve relevant documents when answering questions
