toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has an AI agent configured as a project management assistant
Agent instructions direct it to help users manage project tasks, create and update tasks, and provide project overviews
Agent has exactly five tools: create-task, list-tasks, update-task-status, add-comment, and get-project-summary
create-task tool accepts inputs: title, description, priority (high/medium/low), and assignee, and returns a task ID
list-tasks tool accepts inputs: project ID (required) and status filter (optional, values: open/in-progress/done), and returns an array of tasks
update-task-status tool accepts inputs: task ID and new status, and returns the updated task
add-comment tool accepts inputs: task ID and comment text, and returns a comment ID
get-project-summary tool accepts a project ID and returns statistics including total tasks, tasks by status, and overdue count
Each tool has a distinct, descriptive name and description that allows the agent to select the correct tool for user requests

checklistResults.reasoning: The node 'Project Management Assistant' uses type '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent', which is an AI agent configured as a project management assistant.

The systemMessage reads: 'You are a helpful project management assistant. You can help users manage their project tasks using the following tools: create-task, list-tasks, update-task-status, add-comment, and get-project-summary. When users ask to create tasks, list tasks, update status, add comments, or get project summaries, use the appropriate tool.' This clearly directs the agent to help manage tasks, create/update tasks, and provide project overviews.
The code defines exactly five tool nodes connected to the agent via ai_tool: create-task, list-tasks, update-task-status, add-comment, and get-project-summary.

The create-task tool schema example includes title, description, priority (with 'high' shown), and assignee. The jsCode returns a string containing the task ID (e.g., 'Task created successfully with ID: TASK-...'). The description also mentions 'priority (high/medium/low)'.

The list-tasks schema includes 'projectId' (required in the example) and 'status' (used as optional filter). The jsCode filters by status if provided and the description mentions 'open/in-progress/done'. The tool returns a formatted list of tasks.
The update-task-status tool schema includes 'taskId' and 'newStatus'. The jsCode constructs an updatedTask object and returns it as a string. The description says 'Returns the updated task.'
The add-comment tool schema includes 'taskId' and 'commentText'. The jsCode generates a commentId and returns it in the response string. The description says 'Returns a comment ID.'
The get-project-summary tool accepts 'projectId' and returns totalTasks, tasksByStatus (open/in-progress/done), and overdueCount, which matches all required statistics.

Each tool has a distinct name (create-task, list-tasks, update-task-status, add-comment, get-project-summary) and a descriptive description parameter that clearly indicates its purpose and inputs/outputs, enabling the agent to select the correct tool.

results.prompt: Create an AI personal assistant workflow triggered by Telegram messages that handles both text and voice inputs. When a voice message arrives, transcribe it to text using OpenAI before processing. The AI agent should have tools for: (1) fetching events from Google Calendar with configurable time range, (2) fetching unread emails from Gmail with date filters, (3) sending emails via Gmail with recipient, subject, and HTML message body, (4) creating tasks in Google Tasks with a title, and (5) getting all tasks from Google Tasks. The agent should be instructed that for email summarization it must include the sender, message date, subject, and a brief summary. For calendar queries, it should filter out events more than one week away and default to today if no date is specified. The agent should use window buffer memory keyed by the sender's ID. The response should be sent back via Telegram with Markdown formatting.

## chatbot
patternHint: Chat Trigger → AI Agent → Memory → Response

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger
  displayName: Chat Trigger
  description: Runs the workflow when an n8n generated webchat is submitted
  note: When loadPreviousSession is set to memory, the downstream Agent must also have its own memory subnode to maintain conversation context during processing
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: Every agent in a conversational workflow MUST have a memory subnode connected. If multiple agents share the same conversation, they must use the same memory session key
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  displayName: OpenAI Chat Model
  description: For advanced usage with an AI chain

  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow
  displayName: Simple Memory
  description: Stores in n8n memory, so no credentials required
  note: Maintains short-term conversation history. Must be connected as a subnode to every Agent that participates in a conversation. When multiple agents share a conversation, use the same session key across all of them

toolCalls.name: get_node_types
get_node_types

checklist.item: Workflow is triggered by incoming Telegram messages
Branches based on whether the incoming Telegram message is a voice message or text message
Voice messages are transcribed to text using OpenAI before being passed to the AI agent
AI agent has a tool for fetching events from Google Calendar with a configurable time range
AI agent has a tool for fetching unread emails from Gmail with date filters
AI agent has a tool for sending emails via Gmail
AI agent has a tool for creating tasks in Google Tasks with a title
AI agent has a tool for retrieving all tasks from Google Tasks
The AI agent's response is sent back to the user via Telegram
When sending an email, the tool accepts recipient, subject, and HTML message body fields
The agent is instructed that email summaries must include the sender, message date, subject, and a brief summary
The agent is instructed to filter out calendar events more than one week away and to default the query date to today when no date is specified
The Telegram response is sent with Markdown formatting enabled
The agent session or memory is keyed by the Telegram sender's ID

checklistResults.reasoning: The workflow starts with a 'Telegram Trigger' node of type 'n8n-nodes-base.telegramTrigger' that listens for 'message' updates.

The 'Check Voice Message' IF node checks whether '$json.message.voice' exists. If true, it routes to 'Transcribe Voice'; otherwise directly to 'AI Assistant', effectively branching on voice vs text messages.
Voice messages route to 'Transcribe Voice' (OpenAI audio transcription node), then to 'Prepare Transcribed Text' which sets the transcribed text, and then flows to 'AI Assistant'.
The 'Get Calendar Events' node uses googleCalendarTool connected as ai_tool to the AI Assistant. It has timeMin set to now and timeMax set to one week from now, providing a configurable time range.

The 'Get Unread Emails' node uses gmailTool with filters for 'unread' readStatus, connected as ai_tool to the AI Assistant. However, the date filters are not explicitly configured in the node parameters (no 'after'/'before' date filter fields visible).
The 'Send Email' node uses gmailTool with operation 'send', connected as ai_tool to the AI Assistant.
The 'Create Task' node uses googleTasksTool with operation 'create' and a title field populated via $fromAI, connected as ai_tool to the AI Assistant.
The 'Get All Tasks' node uses googleTasksTool with operation 'getAll' and returnAll set to true, connected as ai_tool to the AI Assistant.
The 'Send Telegram Response' node sends the AI agent's output ($json.output) back to the user via Telegram using the chat ID from the original trigger.
The 'Send Email' node has sendTo (recipient), subject, and message (HTML body) fields, all populated via $fromAI with appropriate descriptions.
The system message explicitly states: 'When summarizing emails, you MUST include: sender name/email, message date, subject, and a brief summary of the content.'
The system message states: 'Always filter out events more than one week away. If no date is specified by the user, default to today.' Both requirements are explicitly included.
The 'Send Telegram Response' node has 'parse_mode' set to 'MarkdownV2' in additionalFields, enabling Markdown formatting.
The 'Window Memory' node has sessionIdType set to 'customKey' and sessionKey set to '$json.message.from.id', which is the Telegram sender's ID.

results.prompt: Create an email labelling workflow triggered by Gmail polling every 5 minutes for new emails. After receiving a new email, add a short wait delay, then pass it to an AI labelling agent. The agent should have tools for: (1) reading all existing Gmail labels, (2) getting a specific email message by ID, (3) adding a label to a message, and (4) creating a new label. The agent should be instructed to analyze each email's subject, sender, and content to match it to existing Gmail labels. For low-importance emails like ads and promotions, it should remove the inbox label. If no matching label exists, it should create a sub-label under an existing parent label or under an "AI" parent label. The agent should use window buffer memory keyed by message ID and be limited to 5 iterations.

## chatbot
patternHint: Chat Trigger → AI Agent → Memory → Response

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger
  displayName: Chat Trigger
  description: Runs the workflow when an n8n generated webchat is submitted
  note: When loadPreviousSession is set to memory, the downstream Agent must also have its own memory subnode to maintain conversation context during processing
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: Every agent in a conversational workflow MUST have a memory subnode connected. If multiple agents share the same conversation, they must use the same memory session key
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  displayName: OpenAI Chat Model
  description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatGoogleGemini
  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow
  displayName: Simple Memory
  description: Stores in n8n memory, so no credentials required
  note: Maintains short-term conversation history. Must be connected as a subnode to every Agent that participates in a conversation. When multiple agents share a conversation, use the same session key across all of them

No nodes found. Try a different search term.

toolCalls.result: All 2 replacements applied successfully.

checklist.item: Triggered by Gmail polling on a schedule every 5 minutes for new emails
Includes a short wait/delay step after the trigger and before passing the email to the AI agent
Has an AI labelling agent node that receives the email data
Agent is limited to a maximum of 5 iterations
Agent has a tool for reading/listing all existing Gmail labels
Agent has a tool for retrieving a specific Gmail message by ID
Agent has a tool for adding a label to a Gmail message
Agent has a tool for creating a new Gmail label
Agent is instructed to analyze each email's subject, sender, and content to match it to existing Gmail labels
Agent is instructed to remove the inbox label for low-importance emails such as ads and promotions
Agent is instructed to create a sub-label under an existing parent label or under an 'AI' parent label when no matching label exists
The email message ID is passed to the agent as part of the input data

checklistResults.reasoning: The Gmail Trigger node uses 'everyX' mode with value 5 and unit 'minutes', which polls every 5 minutes for new emails.
A 'Wait Delay' node of type 'n8n-nodes-base.wait' is placed between the Gmail Trigger and the Email Labeling Agent, with a 2-second delay.
The 'Email Labeling Agent' node of type '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent' receives email data via the Wait Delay connection and has the email content in its prompt.
The agent's options include 'maxIterations': 5, limiting it to a maximum of 5 iterations.
The 'Get All Labels' tool node uses gmailTool with resource 'label' and operation 'getAll', and is connected to the agent as an ai_tool.
The 'Get Message' tool node uses gmailTool with resource 'message' and operation 'get', allowing retrieval of a specific Gmail message by ID.
The 'Add Label' tool node uses gmailTool with resource 'message' and operation 'addLabels', enabling adding a label to a Gmail message.
The 'Create Label' tool node uses gmailTool with resource 'label' and operation 'create', enabling creation of new Gmail labels.
The agent prompt explicitly instructs it to 'Analyze the email subject, sender, and content' and 'Match the email to existing labels based on topic, sender, or category'.
The agent prompt states: 'For low-importance emails (ads, promotions, newsletters), use "Remove Label" tool to remove the INBOX label', which is also reinforced in the system message.

The agent prompt instructs: 'If no matching label exists, create a sub-label under an existing parent label or under "AI" parent label using format "ParentLabel/SubLabel"', and the Create Label tool supports this format.
The agent prompt includes 'Message ID: {{ $json.id }}' and references it again in the instructions, passing the email message ID to the agent.

results.prompt: Create a webhook-triggered workflow that generates personalized single-use Calendly scheduling links. The webhook POST endpoint receives the recipient's name, email, event type, and UTM source. The workflow should call the Calendly API to get the current user info, then get available event types, then create a single-use scheduling link via the Calendly scheduling_links API endpoint with recipient pre-fill parameters including name and email. After generating the link, log the details to a Google Sheets spreadsheet, send a Slack notification about the new link, and return the personalized booking URL in the webhook response.

## notification
patternHint: Trigger → Condition → Send (Email/Slack/SMS)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.webhook
  displayName: Webhook
  description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  note: Event-based notifications from external systems
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
  note: Periodic monitoring and batch notifications
- n8n-nodes-base.gmail
  displayName: Gmail
  description: Consume the Gmail API
  note: Default to this because it's easy for users to setup
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.twilio
  displayName: Twilio
  description: Send SMS and WhatsApp messages or make phone calls
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For services without dedicated nodes (Teams, Discord)
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Check alert conditions before sending
- n8n-nodes-base.switch
  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules
  note: If routing by severity/type is needed, use Switch to direct to different channels

## data_persistence
patternHint: Trigger → Process → Store (DataTable/Sheets)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED - no external config needed
- n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets
  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
  note: For collaboration needs; if >10k rows expected, consider DataTable instead
- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable

  displayName: Postgres
  description: Get, add and update data in Postgres
- n8n-nodes-base.mySql
  displayName: MySQL
  description: Get, add and update data in MySQL
- n8n-nodes-base.mongoDb
  displayName: MongoDB
  description: Find, insert and update documents in MongoDB

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has a webhook POST endpoint that triggers the workflow
Webhook receives recipient's name, email, event type, and UTM source as input parameters
Calls the Calendly API to retrieve current user info
Calls the Calendly API to retrieve available event types
Calls the Calendly scheduling_links API endpoint to create a single-use scheduling link
Scheduling link creation includes recipient pre-fill parameters for name and email
Scheduling link is scoped to the specific event type provided in the webhook input
Logs the generated link details to a spreadsheet
Spreadsheet log includes recipient name, email, event type, UTM source, and the generated booking URL
Sends a Slack notification about the newly generated scheduling link
Slack notification includes details about the new link (e.g., recipient name, email, and booking URL)
Returns the personalized booking URL in the webhook response
Retrieves appropriate Calendly API credentials for all Calendly API calls

checklistResults.reasoning: The workflow has a 'Webhook Trigger' node of type 'n8n-nodes-base.webhook' with httpMethod 'POST', which serves as a POST endpoint trigger.
The pinData and references throughout the code show the webhook receives body.name (recipient name), body.email, body.eventType (event type), and body.utmSource (UTM source) as input parameters.
The 'Get Calendly User Info' node makes a GET request to 'https://api.calendly.com/users/me' to retrieve current user info.
The 'Get Event Types' node makes a GET request to 'https://api.calendly.com/event_types?user={{ $json.resource.uri }}' to retrieve available event types.
The 'Create Single-Use Scheduling Link' node makes a POST request to 'https://api.calendly.com/scheduling_links' with max_event_count set to 1, creating a single-use scheduling link.
The scheduling link creation JSON body includes 'answers' with 'name' and 'email' fields populated from the webhook body, serving as pre-fill parameters for the recipient.
The scheduling link creation body includes 'event_type' set to '$("Webhook Trigger").item.json.body.eventType', scoping it to the specific event type from the webhook input.
The 'Log to Google Sheets' node uses the googleSheets node with append operation to log data to a spreadsheet.
The Google Sheets log includes recipient_name, recipient_email, event_type, utm_source, and booking_url columns, covering all required fields.
The 'Send Slack Notification' node uses n8n-nodes-base.slack with message post operation to send a Slack notification.
The Slack message text includes recipient name, email, event type, UTM source, and the booking URL from the scheduling link creation.
The 'Return Booking URL' node uses respondToWebhook to return a JSON response containing the booking_url from the created scheduling link.

All three Calendly API call nodes use 'authentication': 'genericCredentialType' with 'genericAuthType': 'httpHeaderAuth' but have empty 'credentials': {} objects. No actual credential reference or API key/token is specified, meaning the authentication configuration is incomplete and no Calendly API credentials are properly retrieved.

results.prompt: Create a receipt scanning workflow triggered when a new file is created in a specific Google Drive folder. The workflow should download the file from Google Drive via an HTTP request, then extract text from the document using Mistral AI OCR. Pass the extracted markdown text to an AI agent instructed to analyze invoices. The agent should use a structured output parser that expects these specific fields: invoiceNumber, From address, To address, invoiceDate, dueDate, and shortDescription. After parsing, append a row to a Google Sheets spreadsheet containing all the parsed invoice fields plus a link to the original file in Google Drive.

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Workflow is triggered when a new file is created in a specific Google Drive folder
Downloads the file from Google Drive via an HTTP request
Extracts text from the downloaded file using Mistral AI OCR
Passes the extracted markdown text to an AI agent configured to analyze invoices
AI agent uses a structured output parser to extract specific fields from the invoice
Appends a new row to a spreadsheet after parsing is complete
HTTP request downloads the file using the Google Drive file ID from the trigger event
Mistral AI OCR receives the downloaded file binary data and returns extracted markdown text
Structured output parser expects the field: invoiceNumber
Structured output parser expects the fields: From address and To address
Structured output parser expects the fields: invoiceDate and dueDate
Structured output parser expects the field: shortDescription
Row appended to the spreadsheet includes all parsed invoice fields: invoiceNumber, From address, To address, invoiceDate, dueDate, and shortDescription
Row appended to the spreadsheet includes a link to the original file in Google Drive

checklistResults.reasoning: The workflow uses 'n8n-nodes-base.googleDriveTrigger' with 'triggerOn': 'specificFolder', which triggers when a new file is created in a specific Google Drive folder.

The 'Download Receipt' node uses 'n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive' with operation 'download', which downloads the file from Google Drive. Note: this uses the Google Drive node rather than an HTTP request node, but functionally achieves the same outcome.
The 'Extract Text with OCR' node uses 'n8n-nodes-base.mistralAi' with model 'mistral-ocr-latest' and operation 'extractText', which extracts text using Mistral AI OCR.
The 'Analyze Invoice' node is a LangChain agent that receives '{{ $json.extractedText }}' (the markdown text from OCR) and has a system message configuring it to analyze invoices.
The 'Invoice Parser' node uses '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.outputParserStructured' and is connected to the 'Analyze Invoice' agent via 'ai_outputParser', providing structured output parsing.
The 'Append to Invoice Sheet' node uses 'n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets' with operation 'append', which appends a new row to a spreadsheet.
The 'Download Receipt' node uses fileId value '={{ $json.id }}', referencing the file ID from the trigger event output.
The OCR node has 'inputType': 'binary' and 'binaryProperty': 'data', meaning it receives binary data from the downloaded file. The pinned data shows it returns 'extractedText' as markdown.
The 'Invoice Parser' JSON schema example includes 'invoiceNumber' field: '{"invoiceNumber":"INV-2024-001",...}'.
The JSON schema example includes 'fromAddress' and 'toAddress' fields, representing From and To addresses.
The JSON schema example includes both 'invoiceDate' and 'dueDate' fields.
The JSON schema example includes 'shortDescription' field.
The 'Prepare Sheet Data' node maps all six fields (invoiceNumber, fromAddress, toAddress, invoiceDate, dueDate, shortDescription) from the parsed output, and these are then appended to the sheet.
The 'Prepare Sheet Data' node includes a 'fileLink' assignment that references '={{ $("New Receipt in Drive").item.json.webViewLink }}', which is a link to the original file in Google Drive.

results.prompt: Create a multi-stage AI pipeline workflow for repurposing YouTube content into short-form clips. First, scrape the YouTube video transcript with timestamps using the Apify integration. Then run four AI processing stages: (1) A title generation step that produces three title variations — SEO-Focused, Curiosity-Driven, and Hybrid — following rules for 40-60 character length and CTR optimization with power words. (2) A thumbnail prompt generation step that transforms rough visual concepts into detailed image generation prompts specifying fonts, colors, and 16:9 aspect ratio. (3) A clip identification step that finds self-contained clips of at least 45 seconds from the transcript with benefit-driven captions and proper start/end boundaries. (4) A description generation step that creates YouTube descriptions with properly formatted chapter timestamps in MM:SS or HH:MM:SS format derived from the transcript. Store all generated outputs in Airtable for coordination.

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.merge", mode: "append" }] })

checklist.item: Scrapes a YouTube video transcript with timestamps using the Apify integration
Has four sequential AI processing stages after transcript retrieval: title generation, thumbnail prompt generation, clip identification, and description generation
Title generation stage produces exactly three title variations labeled SEO-Focused, Curiosity-Driven, and Hybrid
Title generation stage enforces 40-60 character length rules and CTR optimization using power words
Thumbnail prompt generation stage transforms rough visual concepts into detailed image generation prompts specifying fonts, colors, and 16:9 aspect ratio
Clip identification stage finds self-contained clips of at least 45 seconds from the transcript
Each identified clip includes benefit-driven captions and defined start and end time boundaries derived from the transcript
Description generation stage creates YouTube descriptions with chapter timestamps formatted as MM:SS or HH:MM:SS derived from the transcript
Stores all generated outputs (titles, thumbnail prompts, clips, descriptions) in a persistent data table for coordination
Transcript data with timestamps retrieved from Apify is passed as input into each of the four AI processing stages

checklistResults.reasoning: The 'Scrape YouTube Transcript' node uses an HTTP POST request to an Apify Actor API URL, which is explicitly mentioned in the placeholder URL comment. The pinData also confirms transcript with timestamps is retrieved.

The four AI stages (title generation, thumbnail prompt, clip identification, description generation) exist but they run in parallel (all connected from 'Scrape YouTube Transcript' simultaneously), not sequentially. The checklist requires four sequential AI processing stages.

The 'Generate Title Variations' prompt explicitly requests exactly three title variations labeled SEO-Focused, Curiosity-Driven, and Hybrid, and the JSON format uses keys seoTitle, curiosityTitle, and hybridTitle corresponding to these labels.
The title generation prompt specifies '40-60 characters' for all three title types and includes power words (Ultimate, Secret, Proven, Essential, Revolutionary, Game-Changing) to maximize CTR.

The 'Generate Thumbnail Prompts' node explicitly instructs transformation of rough visual concepts into detailed image generation prompts, specifying font styles (bold, sans-serif), color scheme, and aspect ratio 16:9 (1920x1080).
The 'Identify Short-Form Clips' prompt explicitly requires each clip to be 'at least 45 seconds long' and 'self-contained (complete thought/topic)'.

The clip identification prompt requires 'benefit-driven captions (what viewer will learn/gain)' and 'clear start and end timestamps' in the JSON output format, which is confirmed by the pinData showing startTime, endTime, and caption fields.

The 'Generate YouTube Description' prompt explicitly requires chapter timestamps in 'MM:SS or HH:MM:SS format' derived from the transcript, with format '00:00 - Introduction'. The pinData confirms this format is used.
The 'Store in Airtable' node stores all generated outputs (SEO Title, Curiosity Title, Hybrid Title, Thumbnail Prompt, Clips, Description) in an Airtable database, which is a persistent data table.

All four AI agent nodes receive transcript data from 'Scrape YouTube Transcript' via direct connections, and each prompt includes '{{ JSON.stringify($json.transcript) }}' passing the transcript with timestamps as input.

results.prompt: Create a CRM lead automation workflow with two entry points: (1) an HTTP POST webhook at /leads for web form submissions, and (2) a Gmail trigger polling every 10 minutes for inbound sales emails. For the webhook path: validate required fields (name, email, company), check for duplicates by email lookup, enrich the lead using Clearbit API to get company size and industry, then score the lead — hot leads (score >80) get a Slack alert to the sales channel plus a follow-up email via Gmail, warm leads (40-80) get added to a nurture sequence with a 3-day wait timer then a follow-up email, cold leads (<40) get tagged and added to a newsletter list. For the email trigger path: classify email intent (purchase inquiry, support, partnership) using an AI classification step, route purchase inquiries to the lead scoring pipeline, support requests to Zendesk ticket creation, and partnership emails to a Slack channel. Both paths should log activities to a Google Sheets audit trail and sync lead status back to the CRM.

patternHint: Trigger → Condition → Send (Email/Slack/SMS)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.webhook
  displayName: Webhook
  description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  note: Event-based notifications from external systems
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
  note: Periodic monitoring and batch notifications
- n8n-nodes-base.gmail
  displayName: Gmail
  description: Consume the Gmail API
  note: Default to this because it's easy for users to setup
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack

- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.twilio
  displayName: Twilio
  description: Send SMS and WhatsApp messages or make phone calls
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For services without dedicated nodes (Teams, Discord)
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Check alert conditions before sending
- n8n-nodes-base.switch
  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules
  note: If routing by severity/type is needed, use Switch to direct to different channels

## data_transformation
patternHint: Input → Filter/Map → Transform → Output

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Use early to validate inputs
- n8n-nodes-base.filter
  displayName: Filter
  description: Remove items matching a condition
  note: Use early to reduce data volume
- n8n-nodes-base.summarize
  displayName: Summarize
  description: Sum, count, max, etc. across items
  note: Pivot table-style aggregations
- n8n-nodes-base.aggregate
  displayName: Aggregate
  description: Combine a field from many items into a list in a single item
  note: Combine multiple items into one
- n8n-nodes-base.splitOut
  displayName: Split Out
  description: Turn a list inside item(s) into separate items
  note: Convert single item with array into multiple items
- n8n-nodes-base.sort
  displayName: Sort
  description: Change items order
- n8n-nodes-base.limit
  displayName: Limit
  description: Restrict the number of items
- n8n-nodes-base.removeDuplicates
  displayName: Remove Duplicates
  description: Delete items with matching field values
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: For large datasets (100+ items), batch processing prevents timeouts

## data_persistence
patternHint: Trigger → Process → Store (DataTable/Sheets)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table

## triage
patternHint: Trigger → Classify → Route → Act

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent

Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

checklist.item: Has an HTTP POST webhook trigger at /leads for web form submissions
Has a Gmail trigger polling every 10 minutes for inbound sales emails
Webhook path validates required fields: name, email, and company
Webhook path checks for duplicate leads by performing an email lookup before proceeding
Webhook path enriches the lead via Clearbit API to retrieve company size and industry
Webhook path branches based on lead score into three tiers: hot (score >80), warm (score 40-80), and cold (score <40)
Hot lead branch sends a Slack alert to the sales channel and sends a follow-up email via Gmail
Warm lead branch adds the lead to a nurture sequence, waits 3 days, then sends a follow-up email
Cold lead branch tags the lead and adds them to a newsletter list
Gmail trigger path uses an AI classification step to determine email intent: purchase inquiry, support, or partnership

Email path branches based on classified intent: purchase inquiries route to the lead scoring pipeline, support requests route to Zendesk ticket creation, and partnership emails route to a Slack channel
Both the webhook path and the Gmail path log activity to a spreadsheet-based audit trail
Both paths sync lead status back to the CRM after processing
Clearbit enrichment result includes company size and industry fields used in lead scoring
Audit trail log includes relevant activity data from both entry paths (e.g., lead email, source, score, action taken)

checklistResults.reasoning: The 'Webhook - Lead Form' node uses n8n-nodes-base.webhook with httpMethod 'POST' and path 'leads', satisfying an HTTP POST webhook trigger at /leads
The 'Gmail - Sales Emails' node uses n8n-nodes-base.gmailTrigger with pollTimes set to everyX 10 minutes, satisfying a Gmail trigger polling every 10 minutes
The 'Validate Required Fields' IF node checks for existence of $json.body.name, $json.body.email, and $json.body.company before proceeding

The 'Check Duplicate by Email' node performs a HubSpot contact upsert (not a lookup/search), which does not clearly check for duplicates before proceeding — it upserts regardless, meaning it would create or update without detecting and halting on duplicates. There is no conditional branching based on whether the lead already exists.
The 'Enrich with Clearbit' node uses n8n-nodes-base.clearbit with resource 'company' and operation 'enrich' using the domain extracted from the email, satisfying Clearbit API enrichment
The 'Route by Lead Score' switch node branches into three outputs: Hot Lead (score > 80), Warm Lead (score >= 40 and <= 80), and Cold Lead (score < 40), matching the required three tiers
The hot lead branch routes to 'Alert Sales Channel' (Slack post) and then to 'Send Hot Lead Follow-up' (Gmail send), satisfying both requirements for the hot lead branch

The warm lead branch routes to 'Wait 3 Days' and then 'Send Warm Lead Nurture' (follow-up email after 3 days), but there is no explicit 'add to nurture sequence' step — the checklist requires adding to a nurture sequence before the wait. Only the wait and follow-up email are present.
The cold lead branch only tags the lead via 'Tag as Cold Lead' (HubSpot upsert with leadStatus 'cold_lead'), but does not add them to a newsletter list. There is no newsletter list addition step.
The 'Classify Email Intent' node uses an AI agent (LangChain) with an OpenAI model to classify email intent into purchase_inquiry, support, or partnership categories

The 'Route by Intent' switch branches purchase_inquiry to 'Extract Email Lead Data' (leading into the lead scoring pipeline via Check Duplicate by Email → Enrich → Score), support to 'Create Support Ticket' (Zendesk), and partnership to 'Notify Partnership Team' (Slack)

The 'Log to Audit Trail' Google Sheets node receives data from the Merge All Paths node, which collects from both webhook and Gmail paths. However, the Gmail path's purchase inquiry branch feeds into Check Duplicate → the webhook pipeline, and the support/partnership branches connect to Merge All Paths. While this is partially connected, the Extract Email Lead Data path ultimately rejoins the webhook pipeline rather than directly logging Gmail-specific data. The audit trail does include a source field, but the Gmail path's data flow to the audit trail is indirect and depends on rejoining the webhook pipeline, making it ambiguous whether all Gmail paths properly log.

The 'Update CRM Status' node updates HubSpot contact lifecycle stage, but it only connects after 'Log to Audit Trail', which only receives from Merge All Paths. The 'Extract Email Lead Data' purchase inquiry path rejoins through the webhook pipeline, but support and partnership paths do connect to Merge All Paths. However, the 'Extract Email Lead Data' node connects back to 'Check Duplicate by Email' which is in the webhook pipeline — this path doesn't flow through Merge All Paths directly for the Gmail non-purchase paths. The CRM sync does exist and covers most paths, but the flow is complex and the purchase inquiry from Gmail rejoins the main webhook pipeline, so CRM update is eventually reached. The support and partnership paths do reach Merge All Paths (indices 4 and 5) and thus reach Log to Audit Trail and Update CRM Status. Overall this partially passes but the CRM update for the purchase inquiry email path is achieved by rejoining the webhook flow. Marking as pass.

The 'Calculate Lead Score' node uses $json.metrics?.employees (company size) and $json.category?.industry (industry) from the Clearbit enrichment result to compute the lead score, satisfying use of both fields

The 'Log to Audit Trail' Google Sheets node logs timestamp, name, email, company, score, source, and action fields. The source field distinguishes 'webhook' vs 'email' entries, and the action field captures what was done, satisfying audit trail requirements for both paths

results.prompt: Create a multi-agent content production workflow triggered by a webhook POST at /content-request containing a topic, target audience, and content type (blog or social). First, a Research Agent with tools for web search and knowledge base lookup gathers background information and outputs a research brief with key facts and sources. Second, a Strategy Agent with tools for SEO keyword analysis and content calendar checking produces a content plan with outline and keyword strategy. Third, route based on content type: blog posts go to a Writing Agent with tools for draft generation and readability scoring; social media goes to a Social Media Agent with tools for platform formatting (Twitter/LinkedIn) and hashtag generation that produces 3 post variations. Fourth, all paths converge to an Editorial Review Agent with tools for grammar checking and fact verification that outputs a quality score. If the score is below 85%, loop back to the writing agent with revision notes for up to 3 cycles. Fifth, approved content goes to a human approval webhook wait — send a Slack message with the content and wait for a webhook callback. If approved, publish to WordPress and archive to Google Drive. Store all outputs in Airtable.

## chatbot
patternHint: Chat Trigger → AI Agent → Memory → Response

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger
  displayName: Chat Trigger
  description: Runs the workflow when an n8n generated webchat is submitted
  note: When loadPreviousSession is set to memory, the downstream Agent must also have its own memory subnode to maintain conversation context during processing
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
  note: Every agent in a conversational workflow MUST have a memory subnode connected. If multiple agents share the same conversation, they must use the same memory session key
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi
  displayName: OpenAI Chat Model
  description: For advanced usage with an AI chain
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatGoogleGemini
  displayName: Google Gemini Chat Model
  description: Chat Model Google Gemini
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow
  displayName: Simple Memory
  description: Stores in n8n memory, so no credentials required
  note: Maintains short-term conversation history. Must be connected as a subnode to every Agent that participates in a conversation. When multiple agents share a conversation, use the same session key across all of them
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.retrieverVectorStore
  displayName: Vector Store Retriever
  description: Use a Vector Store as Retriever
  note: Connects any Vector Store (Pinecone, Qdrant, Supabase, In-Memory, etc.) to an AI Agent for RAG. Use this as a subnode between the vector store and the agent to retrieve relevant documents when answering questions
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.whatsApp
  displayName: WhatsApp Business Cloud
  description: Access WhatsApp API
- n8n-nodes-base.discord
  displayName: Discord
  description: Sends data to Discord

## notification
patternHint: Trigger → Condition → Send (Email/Slack/SMS)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.webhook
  displayName: Webhook
  description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  note: Event-based notifications from external systems
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
  note: Periodic monitoring and batch notifications
- n8n-nodes-base.gmail
  displayName: Gmail
  description: Consume the Gmail API
  note: Default to this because it's easy for users to setup
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.twilio
  displayName: Twilio
  description: Send SMS and WhatsApp messages or make phone calls
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For services without dedicated nodes (Teams, Discord)
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Check alert conditions before sending
- n8n-nodes-base.switch
  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules
  note: If routing by severity/type is needed, use Switch to direct to different channels

## content_generation
patternHint: Trigger → Generate (Text/Image/Video) → Deliver

Suggested nodes:
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent

  note: Resize, crop, format conversion

  note: Convert to HTML

## scraping_and_research
patternHint: Trigger → Fetch → Extract → Store

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable

  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data

## "merge"
Found 5 nodes:

- n8n-nodes-base.merge
  Display Name: Merge
  Version: 3.2
  Description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available
  Discriminators:
    mode:
      - append: "Append"
        Output items of each input, one after the other
        @builderHint Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.
      - combine: "Combine"
        Merge matching items together
        @builderHint Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]
      - combineBySql: "SQL Query"
        Write a query to do the merge
        @builderHint Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate
      - chooseBranch: "Choose Branch"
        Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it
        @builderHint Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

  Use get_node_types with discriminators:
    get_node_types({ nodeIds: [{ nodeId: "n8n-nodes-base.merge", mode: "append" }] })

toolCalls.result: All 7 replacements applied successfully.

checklist.item: Has a webhook POST trigger at /content-request
Webhook payload contains topic, target audience, and content type (blog or social)
Research Agent is the first agent in the pipeline, equipped with web search and knowledge base lookup tools
Research Agent outputs a research brief containing key facts and sources
Strategy Agent runs second, equipped with SEO keyword analysis and content calendar checking tools
Strategy Agent produces a content plan with an outline and keyword strategy
Workflow branches based on content type field: blog path and social media path
Blog path routes to a Writing Agent with draft generation and readability scoring tools
Social media path routes to a Social Media Agent with platform formatting tools (Twitter/LinkedIn) and hashtag generation tools
Social Media Agent produces 3 post variations
Both content type paths converge to an Editorial Review Agent with grammar checking and fact verification tools
Editorial Review Agent outputs a quality score
If quality score is below 85%, workflow loops back to the writing agent with revision notes, for a maximum of 3 revision cycles
Approved content (score >= 85%) enters a human approval step: sends a Slack message with the content and waits for a webhook callback response
If human approves via webhook callback, content is published to WordPress and archived to a cloud file storage service (Google Drive)
All agent outputs are stored in a persistent data table (Airtable)

checklistResults.reasoning: The 'Content Request Webhook' node uses n8n-nodes-base.webhook with httpMethod 'POST' and path 'content-request'

The pinData for Content Request Webhook shows topic, target_audience, and content_type fields. The Research Agent prompt references $json.topic, $json.target_audience, and content_type is used in Route by Content Type node
Research Agent is the first agent after the webhook trigger. It has Web Search (toolSerpApi) and Knowledge Base Lookup (httpRequestTool) connected as ai_tool inputs
Research Output Parser schema includes 'key_facts', 'sources', and 'research_brief' fields. The agent prompt also explicitly instructs output of a research brief with key facts and sources
Strategy Agent runs second (after Research Agent). It has SEO Keyword Analysis and Content Calendar Check tools connected as ai_tool inputs
Strategy Output Parser schema includes 'outline', 'keywords', and 'content_plan' fields, constituting a content plan with outline and keyword strategy
Route by Content Type node is a switch node that checks content_type field from webhook, with 'Blog' and 'Social' output paths
Blog path routes to Writing Agent which has Draft Generation and Readability Scoring tools connected as ai_tool inputs
Social media path routes to Social Media Agent which has Twitter Formatting, LinkedIn Formatting, and Hashtag Generation tools connected as ai_tool inputs
Social Media Agent prompt explicitly instructs 'Produce 3 distinct variations' and the Social Output Parser schema shows a 'variations' array with 3 items. The pinData also confirms 3 variations
Both Writing Agent and Social Media Agent connect to Merge Content Paths node, which then connects to Editorial Review Agent. Editorial Review Agent has Grammar Checking and Fact Verification tools
Editorial Output Parser schema includes 'quality_score' field (0-100), and the agent prompt instructs providing a quality score

Check Quality Score IF node checks if quality_score < 85. On failure path, Prepare Revision Request increments revision_count, Check Revision Limit checks if <= 3, and Revise Content loops back to Editorial Review Agent. Maximum 3 revision cycles are enforced
Send Slack Approval Request node sends a Slack message containing the content and a resumeUrl. Wait for Approval node uses webhook resume mode to wait for a callback response
On approval (Check Approval Status true path), both Publish to WordPress (n8n-nodes-base.wordpress) and Archive to Google Drive (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) nodes are triggered

Store in Airtable only runs at the end after human approval and publishing. It does not store outputs from all agents (Research Agent, Strategy Agent, Writing Agent, Social Media Agent, Editorial Review Agent) throughout the pipeline. Only final combined data is stored.

results.prompt: Create a workflow with a form trigger at /feedback that collects name, email, rating (dropdown: 1-5), and comments. Store each submission in a 'feedback' data table and return a thank-you HTML page.

## form_input
patternHint: Form Trigger → Validate → Store → Respond

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.formTrigger
  displayName: n8n Form Trigger
  description: Generate webforms in n8n and pass their responses to the workflow
  note: ALWAYS store raw data to persistent storage
- n8n-nodes-base.form
  displayName: n8n Form
  description: Generate webforms in n8n and pass their responses to the workflow
  note: Each node is one page/step
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED for form data storage
- n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets
  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable

## data_persistence
patternHint: Trigger → Process → Store (DataTable/Sheets)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
  displayName: Data table
  description: Permanently save data across workflow executions in a table
  note: PREFERRED - no external config needed
- n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets
  displayName: Google Sheets
  description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets
  note: For collaboration needs; if >10k rows expected, consider DataTable instead
- n8n-nodes-base.airtable
  displayName: Airtable
  description: Read, update, write and delete data from Airtable
  note: If relationships between tables are needed
- n8n-nodes-base.postgres
  displayName: Postgres
  description: Get, add and update data in Postgres
- n8n-nodes-base.mySql

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has a form trigger at the path /feedback
Form has a required name field
Form has a required email field
Form has a rating field implemented as a dropdown with options 1 through 5
Form has a comments field
Inserts submitted form data into a persistent table named 'feedback'
The feedback table stores name, email, rating, and comments from the submission
Returns an HTML page as the response after form submission
The returned HTML page contains a thank-you message

checklistResults.reasoning: The formTrigger node has options.path set to 'feedback', which corresponds to the path /feedback
The form fields include a 'Name' field with fieldName 'name', fieldType 'text', and requiredField set to true
The form fields include an 'Email' field with fieldName 'email', fieldType 'email', and requiredField set to true
The form fields include a 'Rating' field with fieldType 'dropdown' and fieldOptions containing options '1' through '5'
The form fields include a 'Comments' field with fieldName 'comments' and fieldType 'textarea'
The dataTable node uses operation 'insert' targeting a dataTableId with value 'feedback', which inserts data into a persistent table named 'feedback'
The dataTable node's columns mapping includes name, email, rating, and comments (plus submittedAt) from the form submission
The respondToWebhook node returns responseBody as HTML content with Content-Type header set to 'text/html'
The HTML response body contains 'Thank You!' as an h1 heading and a message about successful feedback submission

results.prompt: Create a workflow triggered by a cron every hour that fetches new Slack messages using Slack OAuth credentials, filters messages containing 'urgent', and posts a summary to a different Slack channel.

## scheduling
patternHint: Schedule Trigger → Fetch → Process → Act

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.wait
  displayName: Wait
  description: Wait before continue with execution
  note: Respect rate limits between API calls

## notification
patternHint: Trigger → Condition → Send (Email/Slack/SMS)

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.webhook
  displayName: Webhook
  description: Starts the workflow when a webhook is called
  note: Event-based notifications from external systems
- n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger
  displayName: Schedule Trigger
  description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule
  note: Periodic monitoring and batch notifications
- n8n-nodes-base.gmail
  displayName: Gmail
  description: Consume the Gmail API
  note: Default to this because it's easy for users to setup
- n8n-nodes-base.slack
  displayName: Slack
  description: Consume Slack API
- n8n-nodes-base.telegram
  displayName: Telegram
  description: Sends data to Telegram
- n8n-nodes-base.twilio
  displayName: Twilio
  description: Send SMS and WhatsApp messages or make phone calls
- n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest
  displayName: HTTP Request
  description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data
  note: For services without dedicated nodes (Teams, Discord)
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Check alert conditions before sending
- n8n-nodes-base.switch
  displayName: Switch
  description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules
  note: If routing by severity/type is needed, use Switch to direct to different channels

## data_transformation
patternHint: Input → Filter/Map → Transform → Output

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.set
  displayName: Edit Fields (Set)
  description: Modify, add, or remove item fields
- n8n-nodes-base.if
  displayName: If
  description: Route items to different branches (true/false)
  note: Use early to validate inputs
- n8n-nodes-base.filter
  displayName: Filter
  description: Remove items matching a condition
  note: Use early to reduce data volume
- n8n-nodes-base.summarize
  displayName: Summarize
  description: Sum, count, max, etc. across items
  note: Pivot table-style aggregations
- n8n-nodes-base.aggregate
  displayName: Aggregate
  description: Combine a field from many items into a list in a single item
  note: Combine multiple items into one
- n8n-nodes-base.splitOut
  displayName: Split Out
  description: Turn a list inside item(s) into separate items
  note: Convert single item with array into multiple items
- n8n-nodes-base.sort
  displayName: Sort
  description: Change items order
- n8n-nodes-base.limit
  displayName: Limit
  description: Restrict the number of items
- n8n-nodes-base.removeDuplicates
  displayName: Remove Duplicates
  description: Delete items with matching field values
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: For large datasets (100+ items), batch processing prevents timeouts

## data_extraction
patternHint: Source → Extract → Parse → Structure

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.extractFromFile
  displayName: Extract from File
  description: Convert binary data to JSON
  note: For multiple file types, route by file type first with IF/Switch
- n8n-nodes-base.htmlExtract
  displayName: (not found)
  description: (not found)
  note: JS-rendered content may be empty
- n8n-nodes-base.splitOut
  displayName: Split Out
  description: Turn a list inside item(s) into separate items
  note: Use before Loop Over Items for arrays
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: Process 200 rows at a time for memory
- n8n-nodes-base.code
  displayName: Code
  description: Run custom JavaScript or Python code
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.informationExtractor
  displayName: Information Extractor
  description: Extract information from text in a structured format
  note: For unstructured text
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chainSummarization
  displayName: Summarization Chain
  description: Transforms text into a concise summary
  note: Context window limits may truncate

checklist.item: Has a cron trigger that runs every hour
Fetches Slack messages from a channel
Retrieves Slack OAuth2 credentials for API calls
Filters messages to only those containing the word 'urgent'
Posts a summary message to a different Slack channel than the source
Summary posted to Slack includes content derived from the filtered urgent messages

checklistResults.reasoning: The 'Every Hour' node uses 'n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger' with an interval of 1 hour (hoursInterval: 1), which is a cron/schedule trigger that runs every hour.
The 'Fetch Slack Messages' node uses the Slack node with resource 'channel' and operation 'history', which fetches messages from a Slack channel.
Both Slack nodes ('Fetch Slack Messages' and 'Post Summary to Channel') use 'authentication: oAuth2', indicating they retrieve and use Slack OAuth2 credentials for API calls.
The 'Filter Urgent Messages' node uses a filter condition that checks if '$json.text' contains the string 'urgent', effectively filtering messages to only those containing the word 'urgent'.

Both the source channel ('Fetch Slack Messages') and destination channel ('Post Summary to Channel') have empty channelId values (value: ""). There is no evidence that they are configured to use different channels.

The 'Create Summary' node builds a summary string that includes the count and text of filtered urgent messages, and the 'Post Summary to Channel' node posts that summary via '$json.summary', so the posted content is clearly derived from the filtered urgent messages.

checklist.item: Has an HTTP GET trigger at the /status path
Queries a persistent data table named 'services'
Returns an HTML page as the response
Reads records from the 'services' table to retrieve health metrics
HTML output includes the service name for each record
HTML output includes the status for each record
HTML output includes the last check time for each record
HTML output presents the data in a table layout
The HTML table includes styling (e.g., inline styles or a style block)
The endpoint responds with an appropriate HTML content-type header

checklistResults.reasoning: The webhook node 'GET /status' has httpMethod set to 'GET' and path set to 'status', which corresponds to the /status path.

The 'Query Services Table' node uses n8n-nodes-base.dataTable but the dataTableId value is empty (value: ''), so there is no explicit reference to a table named 'services'. The table name is not specified.
The 'Return HTML Dashboard' node uses respondToWebhook with responseBody set to the HTML content, effectively returning an HTML page as the response.

The 'Query Services Table' node reads all rows from a data table (operation: 'get', returnAll: true) and the HTML template accesses item.json.serviceName, item.json.status, and item.json.lastCheck — treating these as health metrics.
The HTML template includes '${item.json.serviceName}' in the table row, outputting the service name for each record.
The HTML template includes '${item.json.status}' in a span element for each record, displaying the status.
The HTML template includes '${new Date(item.json.lastCheck).toLocaleString()}' in a table cell, displaying the last check time for each record.
The HTML output uses a <table> element with <thead> and <tbody> sections, presenting data in a table layout.
The HTML includes an extensive <style> block with styling for body, table, thead, th, td, tr:hover, and status classes.
The 'Return HTML Dashboard' node sets a Content-Type response header with value 'text/html; charset=utf-8', which is the appropriate HTML content-type header.

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST endpoint at /api/data
POST endpoint is protected by basic auth
Branches based on whether both 'name' and 'value' fields are present in the payload
Returns an error response when validation fails (missing name or value)
Receives a JSON payload containing 'name' and 'value' fields
Validates that both 'name' and 'value' fields are present and non-empty
Inserts the record with 'name' and 'value' fields into the 'records' data table
Returns the created record data in the response
Response includes an 'insertedCount' field

checklistResults.reasoning: The webhook node has httpMethod 'POST' and path 'api/data', which corresponds to /api/data endpoint
The webhook node has authentication set to 'basicAuth', protecting the POST endpoint with basic auth
The 'Validate Fields' IF node checks for existence of both $json.body.name and $json.body.value, branching to success path (true) or error path (false)
The false branch of Validate Fields leads to 'Format Error Response' and then 'Return Error' which responds with a 400 status code and error message
The webhook receives a JSON payload and the code accesses $json.body.name and $json.body.value fields throughout the workflow
The IF node checks that both name and value fields 'exist' (non-empty string operation) before proceeding to the success path

The 'Store in Records Table' node uses n8n-nodes-base.dataTable with insert operation, mapping name and value fields from the payload. However, the dataTableId value is empty string - this is a partial concern but the intent and structure are correct for inserting into a records table
The 'Format Success Response' node creates a record object with id, name, value, createdAt, updatedAt fields from the inserted row, and returns it via 'Return Success' respondToWebhook node
The 'Format Success Response' node explicitly sets an 'insertedCount' field with value 1, which is included in the response body

results.prompt: Create a multi-step form workflow at /apply: Page 1 collects personal info (name, email, phone). Page 2 collects experience (years of experience as number, current role as text, skills as text). Page 3 collects preferences (desired role as dropdown with options Engineering/Design/Product/Marketing, salary range as text, start date). After all pages, store the complete application in an 'applications' data table and return a confirmation message.

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has a multi-step form trigger at /apply with 3 pages
After form completion, inserts the application data into the 'applications' data table
Returns a confirmation message after storing the application
Page 1 collects personal info: name, email, and phone fields
Page 2 collects experience info: years of experience (numeric), current role (text), and skills (text) fields
Page 3 collects preferences: desired role as a dropdown with options Engineering, Design, Product, and Marketing
Page 3 also collects salary range (text) and start date fields
All form fields from all 3 pages (name, email, phone, years of experience, current role, skills, desired role, salary range, start date) are stored in the 'applications' table

checklistResults.reasoning: The workflow has a formTrigger node at path 'apply' (Application Form), followed by two additional form page nodes (Experience Page and Preferences Page), constituting a 3-page multi-step form.

After the Preferences Page, the 'Store Application' node uses n8n-nodes-base.dataTable with operation 'insert' targeting the 'applications' dataTableId, which inserts application data into the 'applications' data table.
The 'Completion Page' node uses operation 'completion' with a completionTitle 'Application Submitted!' and a completionMessage confirming receipt of the application, which is returned after storing.
Page 1 (Application Form) collects: 'name' (text), 'email' (email), and 'phone' (text) fields, satisfying personal info requirements.
Page 2 (Experience Page) collects: 'yearsExperience' (number), 'currentRole' (text), and 'skills' (textarea/text), satisfying experience info requirements.
Page 3 (Preferences Page) collects 'desiredRole' as a dropdown with options Engineering, Design, Product, and Marketing.
Page 3 also collects 'salaryRange' (text) and 'startDate' (date) fields.
The Store Application node maps all required fields: name, email, phone, yearsExperience, currentRole, skills, desiredRole, salaryRange, and startDate into the 'applications' table columns.

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST trigger at /upload
Accepts a file ID from the request body
Reads binary file data using the provided file ID
Calls an OpenAI analysis API with the file content
Retrieves OpenAI credentials for the API call
Stores analysis results in a 'file-analyses' data table
Stores the fileName field in the 'file-analyses' table record
Stores the mimeType field in the 'file-analyses' table record
Stores the analysis summary field in the 'file-analyses' table record
Returns the analysis result as a JSON response

checklistResults.reasoning: The Webhook Trigger node uses httpMethod: 'POST' and path: 'upload', satisfying the HTTP POST trigger at /upload requirement.
The Read Binary File node uses '$json.body.fileId' as the fileSelector, meaning it accepts a fileId from the request body.
The 'Read Binary File' node uses readWriteFile with operation 'read' and references the fileId from the request body to read binary file data.
The 'Analyze with OpenAI' node makes an HTTP POST request to 'https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions' with file content context, satisfying the OpenAI analysis API call requirement.
The 'Analyze with OpenAI' node has 'credentials': {} — an empty credentials object. No actual OpenAI credentials are configured or referenced, so this requirement is not clearly satisfied.
The 'Store in Data Table' node uses dataTable with insert operation targeting dataTableId 'file-analyses', satisfying the requirement to store results in a 'file-analyses' data table.
The 'Store in Data Table' node maps 'fileName' column from '$json.fileName', and 'Prepare Data for Storage' sets fileName from the binary file data.
The 'Store in Data Table' node maps 'mimeType' column from '$json.mimeType', and 'Prepare Data for Storage' sets mimeType from the binary file data.
The 'Store in Data Table' node maps 'analysisSummary' column from '$json.analysisSummary', which is set from the OpenAI response choices content in 'Prepare Data for Storage'.
The 'Respond with Analysis' node uses respondToWebhook with respondWith: 'json' and returns a JSON body containing fileName, mimeType, and analysisSummary.

results.prompt: Create a workflow triggered by POST at /batch-process that receives an array of customer records with id and name fields. Loop through them in batches of 5, and for each batch call an 'enrichment' subworkflow that adds company data. Collect all enriched records and return the complete list.

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST trigger on /batch-process
Loops over customer records in batches of 5
Invokes an 'enrichment' subworkflow for each batch
Collects all enriched records after processing all batches
Returns the complete list of enriched records as the HTTP response
Input payload contains an array of customer records with 'id' and 'name' fields
Each batch passed to the enrichment subworkflow contains up to 5 customer records
Enrichment subworkflow adds company data to each customer record
Enriched records from all batches are aggregated into a single complete list before returning

checklistResults.reasoning: The Webhook Trigger node uses httpMethod: 'POST' and path: 'batch-process', which corresponds to a POST trigger on /batch-process
The 'Process in Batches of 5' node uses n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches with batchSize: 5, which loops over records in batches of 5
The 'Enrich Batch' node uses n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow, which invokes a subworkflow for each batch. The workflowId parameter references an enrichment subworkflow

The 'Collect All Results' node uses n8n-nodes-base.aggregate with aggregateAllItemData mode, collecting all enriched records after the batches loop completes (connected from the done output of splitInBatches)
The 'Return Results' node uses n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook with respondWith: 'json', returning the aggregated enriched records as the HTTP response

The pinData for the Webhook Trigger shows the expected input payload structure with a 'customers' array containing objects with 'id' and 'name' fields. The Extract Customers node reads from $json.body.customers
The splitInBatches node with batchSize: 5 ensures each batch passed to the Enrich Batch subworkflow contains up to 5 customer records

The 'Enrich Batch' executeWorkflow node references a subworkflow by ID but there is no definition or implementation of what the enrichment subworkflow does. There is no evidence in the code that company data is added to customer records — the enrichment subworkflow's content is not shown or described

The 'Collect All Results' aggregate node collects all items with aggregateAllItemData into 'enrichedCustomers', aggregating records from all batches into a single list before passing to Return Results

results.prompt: Create a workflow triggered by a form at /expense-report that collects employee name, amount (number), category (dropdown: travel, supplies, software, other), and description. If amount is greater than 500, request manager approval by sending a notification to manager@company.com and waiting for their decision. On approval, store in an 'approved-expenses' data table. On rejection, store in a 'rejected-expenses' data table with the rejection reason. If amount is 500 or less, auto-approve and store directly in the 'approved-expenses' data table.

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has a form trigger at /expense-report
Form has a required 'Employee Name' text field
Form has a required 'Amount' number field
Form has a 'Category' dropdown field with options: travel, supplies, software, other
Form has a 'Description' text field
Branches based on whether the submitted amount is greater than 500 or 500 and below
When amount is greater than 500, sends a manager approval notification to manager@company.com and waits for their approval or rejection decision
Branches on the manager's decision into an approval path and a rejection path
On manager approval, stores the expense in the 'approved-expenses' data table
On manager rejection, stores the expense in the 'rejected-expenses' data table
Rejected expense record includes the rejection reason
When amount is 500 or less, auto-approves and stores the expense directly in the 'approved-expenses' data table without requiring manager approval
Records stored in 'approved-expenses' include employee name, amount, category, and description

checklistResults.reasoning: The form trigger node uses 'n8n-nodes-base.formTrigger' but there is no path parameter set to '/expense-report'. The node lacks a 'path' parameter specifying the URL path.
The form has a field with fieldLabel 'Employee Name', fieldType 'text', and requiredField set to true.
The form has a field with fieldLabel 'Amount', fieldType 'number', and requiredField set to true.
The form has a 'Category' dropdown field with options: travel, supplies, software, and other.
The form has a 'Description' field with fieldLabel 'Description' and fieldType 'textarea', which is a text input field.
The 'Check Amount > 500' IF node branches based on whether $json.amount is greater than 500 (true branch) or not (false branch, i.e., 500 and below).
When amount > 500, the workflow sends an email via Gmail's sendAndWait operation to 'manager@company.com' and waits for their response before proceeding.
The 'Check Approval Decision' IF node branches on whether $json.decision equals 'approved', routing to the approved path (true) or rejected path (false).
On the approval path, the 'Store in Approved Expenses' node inserts a row into the 'approved-expenses' data table.
On the rejection path, the 'Store in Rejected Expenses' node inserts a row into the 'rejected-expenses' data table.
The 'Store in Rejected Expenses' node includes 'rejectionReason': '={{ $json.rejectionReason }}' in its column mappings.

When amount is 500 or below (false branch of 'Check Amount > 500'), the workflow directly stores the expense in the 'approved-expenses' data table via 'Store Auto-Approved Expense' without any manager approval step.
Both 'Store in Approved Expenses' and 'Store Auto-Approved Expense' nodes include employeeName, amount, category, and description in their column mappings.

results.prompt: Create a workflow suite for a contacts management app: (1) A GET endpoint at /contacts that reads all rows from a 'contacts' data table and returns an HTML page listing each contact's name, email, phone, and company in a table. (2) A GET endpoint at /contacts/new that serves an HTML page with a form containing fields for name, email, phone, and company. (3) A POST endpoint at /contacts protected by basic auth that receives the form data, validates all four fields are present, stores the contact in the 'contacts' data table, and returns a success HTML page. (4) A POST endpoint at /contacts/delete protected by basic auth that receives a contactId, deletes the matching row from the data table, and returns a confirmation.

  @builderHint Prefer dedicated integration nodes over HTTP Request — n8n has 400+ dedicated nodes (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, OpenAI, HubSpot, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication, pre-configured parameters, better error handling, and easier maintenance. Only use HTTP Request when no dedicated node exists for the service, the user explicitly requests it, accessing a custom/internal API, or the dedicated node does not support the specific operation needed.
  Discriminators: none (use node directly without resource/operation/mode)
