checklist.item: Has a GET endpoint at /contacts
Has a GET endpoint at /contacts/new
Has a POST endpoint at /contacts protected by basic auth
Has a POST endpoint at /contacts/delete protected by basic auth
GET /contacts reads all rows from the 'contacts' data table
GET /contacts returns an HTML page listing each contact's name, email, phone, and company in a table
GET /contacts/new returns an HTML page with a form containing fields for name, email, phone, and company
POST /contacts branches based on whether all four fields (name, email, phone, company) are present in the submitted form data
POST /contacts stores the submitted contact (name, email, phone, company) into the 'contacts' data table when validation passes
POST /contacts returns a success HTML page after successfully storing the contact
POST /contacts/delete receives a contactId field from the request
POST /contacts/delete deletes the row matching the provided contactId from the 'contacts' data table
POST /contacts/delete returns a confirmation HTML page or response after deletion

checklistResults.reasoning: There is a webhook node named 'GET /contacts' with httpMethod 'GET' and path 'contacts'
There is a webhook node named 'GET /contacts/new' with httpMethod 'GET' and path 'contacts/new'
There is a webhook node named 'POST /contacts' with httpMethod 'POST', path 'contacts', and authentication 'basicAuth'
There is a webhook node named 'POST /contacts/delete' with httpMethod 'POST', path 'contacts/delete', and authentication 'basicAuth'
The 'Fetch All Contacts' node uses n8n-nodes-base.dataTable with resource 'row', operation 'get', and returnAll true, reading from the contacts data table
The 'Generate Contacts List HTML' node produces an HTML table with columns for Name, Email, Phone, and Company, populated from the fetched contacts data
The 'Generate New Contact Form' node produces an HTML form with input fields for name, email, phone, and company
The 'Validate All Fields Present' IF node checks that $json.body.name, $json.body.email, $json.body.phone, and $json.body.company are all notEmpty, branching based on all four fields
The 'Insert Contact' node uses n8n-nodes-base.dataTable with operation 'insert', mapping name, email, phone, and company from the request body into the contacts data table
After inserting, 'Generate Success HTML' creates a success HTML page and 'Respond Success' returns it as the response
The 'Delete Contact Row' node uses $json.body.contactId as the filter value, indicating it receives a contactId field from the request
The 'Delete Contact Row' node uses dataTable operation 'deleteRows' filtering by id eq $json.body.contactId, deleting the matching row from the contacts table
After deletion, 'Generate Delete Confirmation' creates a confirmation HTML page and 'Respond Delete Confirmation' returns it as the response

results.prompt: Create a document processing pipeline: triggered by POST at /documents with header auth using X-API-Key. The request body contains a fileId. Read the binary file data, then call an 'ocr-extraction' subworkflow using OCR service credentials to extract text. Store the extracted text in a 'documents' data table with the fileName and pageCount. Then loop through each page of extracted text in batches of 3, running a classification step that categorizes the content. Store each classification in a 'classifications' data table with documentId, pageNumber, and category. Finally, return an HTML summary page showing the document name, total page count, and a list of page classifications.

## document_processing
patternHint: Trigger → Extract Text → AI Parse → Store

Suggested nodes:
- n8n-nodes-base.gmailTrigger
  displayName: Gmail Trigger
  description: Fetches emails from Gmail and starts the workflow on specified polling intervals.
- n8n-nodes-base.googleDriveTrigger
  displayName: Google Drive Trigger
  description: Starts the workflow when Google Drive events occur
- n8n-nodes-base.extractFromFile
  displayName: Extract from File
  description: Convert binary data to JSON
  note: Different file types require different operations - route accordingly
- n8n-nodes-base.awsTextract
  displayName: AWS Textract
  description: Sends data to Amazon Textract
  note: For tables and forms in scanned docs
- n8n-nodes-base.mindee
  displayName: Mindee
  description: Consume Mindee API
  note: Specialized invoice/receipt parsing
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent
  displayName: AI Agent
  description: Generates an action plan and executes it. Can use external tools.
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.documentDefaultDataLoader
  displayName: Default Data Loader
  description: Load data from previous step in the workflow
  note: Loads binary files (PDF, CSV, JSON, DOCX, EPUB, text) into LangChain Documents. Auto-detects format from MIME type. Requires a preceding node that outputs binary data
- @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.vectorStoreInMemory
  displayName: Simple Vector Store
  description: The easiest way to experiment with vector stores, without external setup.
  note: No external dependencies needed
- n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches
  displayName: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches)
  description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch
  note: Process 5-10 files at a time

  displayName: Data table

  displayName: Postgres

- n8n-nodes-base.mySql
  displayName: MySQL
  description: Get, add and update data in MySQL
- n8n-nodes-base.mongoDb
  displayName: MongoDB
  description: Find, insert and update documents in MongoDB

toolCalls.name: get_node_types

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST trigger on /documents
POST endpoint is protected by header auth using X-API-Key
Reads binary file data using the fileId from the request body
Invokes an 'ocr-extraction' subworkflow to extract text from the file
Retrieves OCR service credentials for the subworkflow call
Stores extracted text in a 'documents' data table with fileName and pageCount fields
Loops through each page of extracted text in batches of 3
Runs a classification step for each batch that categorizes page content
Stores each page classification in a 'classifications' data table with documentId, pageNumber, and category fields
Returns an HTML page as the final response
HTML summary page includes the document name, total page count, and a list of page classifications

checklistResults.reasoning: The Webhook Trigger node uses httpMethod: 'POST' and path: 'documents', which maps to POST /documents
The Webhook Trigger node has authentication: 'headerAuth', which corresponds to header-based authentication using X-API-Key
The 'Read Binary File' node uses an HTTP GET request with url set to '={{ $json.body.fileId }}', reading the fileId from the request body and fetching binary file data with responseFormat: 'file'
The 'OCR Extraction' node uses type 'n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow' to invoke a subworkflow, with a placeholder indicating it should be the 'ocr-extraction' workflow

There is no node or configuration that retrieves OCR service credentials before or during the subworkflow call. The OCR Extraction node has no credentials configured, and no separate credential-fetching step exists.
The 'Store Document' node uses n8n-nodes-base.dataTable with insert operation and maps fileName and pageCount fields (plus extractedText) into the data table
The 'Batch Pages' node uses n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches with batchSize: 3, which loops through pages in batches of 3
The 'Classify Page' node runs for each batch output from 'Batch Pages' and sends page text to a classification API endpoint that categorizes page content
The 'Store Classification' node inserts into a dataTable with documentId, pageNumber, and category fields mapped from the Classify Page output
The 'Generate HTML Summary' node generates an HTML template, and 'Respond to Webhook' returns it with Content-Type: text/html as the final response
The HTML template includes {{ $json.fileName }} for document name, {{ $json.pageCount }} for total page count, and maps $json.classifications to a table showing pageNumber and category for each page
